# Thread: Making the most of your consult with Dr. Kruse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MJ* — 2012-05-10T21:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-22972>

Since some of you have already had consults, I thought we could use this thread to post ideas on how to make the most of our time with Dr. Kruse. Please share any advice for what you think others should do to prepare, good questions to ask, what information to have at your fingertips etc.,

### indigogirl — 2012-05-10T21:46:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-22975>

MJ,

I had emailed the MM's asking how to figure out what labs I would need before having a consult with Jack.

They said they were working on a package where one could speak to Jack first to discuss health concerns so he could then prescribe labs, and after the labs follow up with us.

### MJ* — 2012-05-10T23:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23001>

> **indigogirl;23056 wrote:** MJ,
>
> I had emailed the MM's asking how to figure out what labs I would need before having a consult with Jack.
>
> They said they were working on a package where one could speak to Jack first to discuss health concerns so he could then prescribe labs, and after the labs follow up with us.

That's awesome, if you get a response on that, can you post it here?

### MJ* — 2012-05-10T23:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23009>

> **August;23060 wrote:** Great topic for a thread, MJ!
>
>
>
> I always go the the doctor with a list of questions and concerns . . . if I am feeling trustful and cooperate, I provide a copy for the doctor . . . I think that would likely be a good trick for making the most of a phone consult type of appointment espeically.
>
>
>
> I guess more obvious . . . provide a copy of recent lab results at the same time that you send over you list of questions . . . .
>
>
>
> and I think in the case of a consult with Dr Kruse . . it would very likely be worth while to get special/specific labs run for the occasion . . . I hope that we can discuss generally what would be some good ones to have done . . . although that is something that has been discussed elsewhere . . so maybe not what this excellent (I can already tell) thread should be all about . . .
>
>
>
> I usually try to plan out what I'm going to say/what I will try to focus on (list or not) . . . but it seems like in my mind a conversation with Dr K might be a difficult thing to plan for . . . I would tend to expect the unexpected!
>
>
>
> Now I have a question . . . Do you "get" a recorded audio of the call afterwards . . . or it is acceptable to record the call yourself . . .I think it would be desirable to be able to listen to it many, many times afterwards!

I brought a list of all the tests and supplements to my doc to discuss, some unfortunately I don't think I'm going to be able to get like the saliva tests because NYS law prohibits it! I have no idea why...how dumb!

For Dr. Kruse I would like to maximize the time and have all the labs he most wants to see...so a list would be great! It would also be really nice if we can report back here what we might have done differently if anything...good question on the audio...

### indigogirl — 2012-05-10T23:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23023>

MJ, Here is my PM to the MM's about a consult and their response:

Originally Posted by indigogirl

Hi MM's,

I would like to make an appt. with Dr Kruse to go over labs...I can give you details of how I'm feeling and where I want to go to be optimal, but would first like to know what labs he would require and what kind of an education pkg I should buy...

This wouldn't be prescribing medicine over the Internet I assume, and he could request specific blood work and based on my labs prescribe supplements and actually give me advice?

Also, how long would I have to wait for an appointment with him?

Thanks!!

We are working on a package to purchase that would allow you to setup a time to go over your history to get a list of labs you may need. Currently, it is not yet available. It should be soon.

You would need to check the calendar for an open time and date. Thanks!

JMM

### MJ* — 2012-05-11T16:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23161>

Excellent, if you can, post here when that package is worked out!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-11T17:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23183>

Best thing to do is provide me with a short narrative of your history......what your top 5 goals or needs are and what you would like to become educated about in the time to help you help yourself and your doctor to get you to optimal......if you do that even without labs I can help you craft a plan of action to use with your doctor.

There are many ways you can use me........just dont make a twitter account using my name OK?

### MJ* — 2012-05-12T01:37:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23296>

> **Jack Kruse;23265 wrote:** Best thing to do is provide me with a short narrative of your history......what your top 5 goals or needs are and what you would like to become educated about in the time to help you help yourself and your doctor to get you to optimal......if you do that even without labs I can help you craft a plan of action to use with your doctor.
>
>
>
> There are many ways you can use me........just dont make a twitter account using my name OK?

LOL LOL that last line...wasn't expecting that...

There are many ways we can use you though, that's good to know! And ok that's a good starting point...

### MJ* — 2012-05-12T01:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23297>

> **August;23251 wrote:** I think some doctors have a set lists of tests that they run . . and then special add on set depending on which conditions you have (or think you might have, if you are looking for a diagnosis) . . I think questionnaires might work the same way (or could) . . .
>
>
>
> MJ . . I'm glad that you mentioned supplements . . . I think in addition to providing labs . . it is important to provide a list of medications and supplements . . in order to provide a complete picture . . .
>
>
>
> That reminds me too . . I used to see a specialist (who was wonderful in many ways although very tradition western medicine in many ways too) . . . she would have me complete several questionnaires before each appointment . . . questions like what supplements and Prescriptions are you taking . . what's better . . what's not better (since you last appointment) . . and questions and concerns to address at the appointment . . . . and then there was another questionnaire that just seemed to be about well being/sleep quality/mood . . that sort of reminded me that it would likely be a good idea to be prepared to talk about sleep quality . . and issues like that . . . things that we might not think to prepare to talk about with other doctors . . . .

Sounds like a great doc August, where'd she go? I agree about supplements...I have lots of questions about those...

### Tizawee — 2012-05-24T14:57:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-27003>

> **MJ\*;23091 wrote: I brought a list of all the tests and supplements to my doc to discuss, some unfortunately I don't think I'm going to be able to get like the saliva tests because NYS law prohibits it! I have no idea why...how dumb!**

**

I just googled "saliva test New York State law." It's the mailing of the saliva that is outlawed, not the tests themselves. One of the sites ( www.hmenterprises.com/Hormonesaliva**.html ) is saying it's now legal. The others say it's not. Ostensibly, you could hand carry the test to your local lab.

Elin

### MJ* — 2012-05-24T16:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-27008>

> **Tizawee;27099 wrote: I just googled "saliva test New York State law." It's the mailing of the saliva that is outlawed, not the tests themselves. One of the sites ( www.hmenterprises.com/Hormonesaliva**.html ) is saying it's now legal. The others say it's not. Ostensibly, you could hand carry the test to your local lab.
>
>
>
> Elin

Thanks so much for looking into this...so I thought the same thing got on line, gave my card to get the test, thinking I would mail it from Conn or NJ, and I got a letter back a couple of days later saying they could not even mail me the test! I did though just find a place in NY that says they have gotten licensing to do the test here, it's about an hour away...I need to call them to find out the details and price and all, unfortunately there's no shopping around...

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-08T21:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-62971>

Bump....

Any other new suggestions or advice?

### caroline — 2012-11-08T21:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-62980>

I am in Australia - 4 months ago I order a saliva test from ZRT. It was confiscated by customs and put in quarantine. Customs wanted it re- documented which ZRT did and then I was charged an extra $75.00 to get it out of quarantine.

I just ordered the same test again today - but beforehand I emailed Canary Club and asked if the test could be sent by US mail instead. They said - no problem and she would watch for my order to come thru. ZRT has great customer service - by the way.

So - we will see if that works better. I am told by other companies shipping to Aus - that shipping by US mail attracts less customs attention. I am keeping my fingers crossed! will update


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Sugar And carb cravings?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-05-11T18:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23193>

Just came off a disastrous hcg round where I was plagued with emotional eating and sugar/carb cravings. I am starting the LR tomorrow. Any advice on how to beat back these God awful cravings and deal with the emotional eating? Would love any help at all. I don't want to gain all my weight back from round one (round two was a wash!). But I almost feel like I am being driven to gain it back. It's awful. Thanks for your suggestions in advance!

### nonchalant — 2012-05-11T21:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23251>

You could try some 5-HTP for a few days. I know it helps for evening cravings, after you have already eaten dinner. It takes 2-3 hours to kick in, though. Helps with low seratonin levels. Long term, though, the LR will fix the cravings. Oh, and CT, for most people, kills cravings even quicker, if you don't have Omega 6 issues.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-11T23:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23255>

PROTEIN FAT PROTEIN FAT PROTEIN FAT.

Any questions?

Okay, so what I mean is look up the leptin reset and follow that. Make sure you get at least 50g protein every morning (and then another full serving at lunch and/or dinner), and don't hold back on the fat. Tell me if a miracle doesn't happen.

(And IIRC, don't ever take 5-HTP if you're on antidepressants, or have been in the last six months.)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T01:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23289>

MamaGrok hits a homerun........

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-12T04:43:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23329>

Reigning Ex-Queen of Cravings, at your service...

### Sem — 2012-05-12T20:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23446>

Thanks MamaGrok. I started the Leptin Reset again today. I had done it with great success before this last round of hcg but I don't think I gave it enough time. I'm going to really give it the full amount of time and not rush into another round. Best to try to deal with all the emotional stuff first. The LR does really help with that. Did you take any supplements like chromium to help? I am going in a couple of weeks to get my full labs. But, from previous labs (and before I really understood the significance), I know that I've had low vitamin D, low magnesium, high ANA, low (but also sometimes hug) steadily and high fsh. They always say my thyroid is normal but now I realize I was probably just doing a tsh. You're quite an inspiration so thanks!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-12T21:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23478>

I didn't take any supplements at all (other than whole foods, like fermented cod liver oil, but nothing geared toward cravings). Getting D & Mg up are a must for everyone. I know I was very low in both before. That ANA means zero wheat; you know that, right? I didn't. It was a shocker to see my ANA.

Keep us posted and let us support you here! It's hard to do alone.

### Sem — 2012-05-12T22:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23481>

I did not know that. Mine was 320. The doctor said he wasn't too concerned since he ran a bunch of other tests for lupus and they supposedly came back negative. Of course he never mentioned wheat! Thankfully, I'm doing it on my own and have switched the whole family over to gluten free. They cried and screamed at first but were fine with it within a week. They even turn down cake at a party because "we don't eat that. It has gluten." yay! Now I have to get them off of other grains and the "gluten free" stuff.  thanks again for all your insight.

### Sem — 2012-05-12T22:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23485>

Anti nuclear antibodies. They're an indicator of autoimmune disease.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-12T23:26:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23505>

> **Sem;23567 wrote:** Anti nuclear antibodies. They're an indicator of autoimmune disease.

Sem, how does one test for them?

### Sem — 2012-05-12T23:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23506>

Indigo girl - it's a blood test. My doctor runs them as part of a standard check up.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-12T23:32:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23507>

So it's an ANA test?

### Sem — 2012-05-13T00:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23524>

Yes, a blood test to test for anti nuclear antibodies.

# Thread: Endicronologist supports Paleo!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ineedanLR — 2012-05-12T00:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/post-23271>

Yesterday, I saw an endicronologist. At the end of the visit, I asked him about Paleo.

As some of you might know, I am deeply disturbed by the American Diabetes Association and their continued support of 60 carbs per meal, with an additional 30 carbs for snacks (~210 carbs per day). There is so much evidence that lower carb limits help diabetics, but still the professional diabetes educators insist on recommending a diet per the ADA guidelines.

So, the endicronologist said lower carbs is better!!! When I asked him if a lower carb diet was going against medical advice, he said NO! He explained that somewhere on the ADA website, there is a disclaimer stating that lower carb diets have been proven to benefit diabetics....but they stop short of actually recommending lower carb intake.

With regard to Paleo, he supported it fully!!!! The elimination of foods known to cause inflammatory responses was his primary reason for supporting it. He also stated that clogging and overloading of the liver with fat interferes with the proper function of the liver and believes the body should burn excess fat in the liver instead of storing it forever. His explanation of the fat burning process sure sounded allot like Dr. J's explanation of detoxification!

The endicronologist was associated with the diabetes clinic of a regional hospital here in Maine.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T01:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/post-23276>

Low the carbs the better? WOW, I hope he does not have a twitter account. That kinda talk can get you in trouble round some parts. LOL

There is so much evidence that lower carb limits help diabetics, but still the professional diabetes educators insist on recommending a diet per the ADA guidelines.

He also stated that clogging and overloading of the liver with fat interferes with the proper function of the liver and believes the body should burn excess fat in the liver instead of storing it forever. His explanation of the fat burning process sure sounded allot like Dr. J's explanation of detoxification!
>

### chocolate — 2012-05-12T01:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23303>

Do you have lithium orotate? Or any vege extracted lithium?

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/05/100521191440.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/05/100521191440.htm)

ScienceDaily (May 21, 2010) — Though it has been prescribed for over 50 years to treat bipolar disorder, there are still many questions regarding exactly how lithium works. However, in a study appearing in this month's Journal of Lipid Research, researchers have provided solid evidence that lithium reduces brain inflammation by adjusting the metabolism of the health-protective omega-3-fatty acid called DHA.

They found that in agreement with some other studies, rats given a six-week lithium treatment had reduced levels of arachidonic acid and its products, which can contribute to inflammation.

In addition, they also demonstrated, for the first time, that lithium treatment increased levels of a metabolite called 17-OH-DHA in response to inflammation. 17-OH-DHA is formed from the omega-3 fatty acid DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) and is the precursor to a wide range of anti-inflammatory compounds known as docosanoids. **Other anti-inflammatory drugs, like aspirin, are known to also enhance docosanoids in their mode of action.**

_My kid has incredible brain inflammation, getting better every day, but as soon as she took lithium, she said she felt like she was first born. When I take the orotate, I get on a really natural mellow sleepy feeling. Like when you are a little kid. It is easy to control. If you get stiff knees back off. You've had enough._

The lithium orotate is a vitamin found in centrum. It takes in about 30 minutes, they are in little tiny 10mgs. People just take one at at time until they figure them out. Usually three or four and then 10 mg weeks later if you think it might help. Or for sleep, 10 mg or 10 mg 2 nights in a row and then again when your sleep is short again. I learned the hard way, I found my self lazy in the most perfect way from it and it took a couple of days for my energy to get high again. The problem/advantage is that it doesn't feel like anything at all. They are an extract of heavy feeders in lithium rich soils. Lots of natural sources, lemon cravers are usually lithium deficient.

BTW...Get well soon Souldanzer!

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T05:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23809>

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0161813X07000496](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0161813X07000496)

Manganese induces cell swelling in cultured astrocytes

Abstract

Manganese in excess is neurotoxic and causes a CNS disorder that resembles Parkinson's disease (manganism). Manganese highly accumulates in astrocytes, which renders these cells more vulnerable to its toxicity. Consistent with this vulnerability, manganese has been shown to cause histopathological changes in astrocytes (Alzheimer type II change), generates oxidative stress and bring about mitochondrial dysfunction, including the induction of the mitochondrial permeability transition (mPT) in astrocytes. In addition to manganism, increased brain levels of manganese have been found in hepatic encephalopathy, a changes are also observed. As low-grade brain edema, possibly secondary to astrocyte swelling, has been reported in hepatic encephalopathy, we hypothesized that manganese may contribute to such edema. We therefore exposed cultured astrocytes to manganese (Mn3+) acetate (25 and 50 μM) for different time periods and examined for changes in cell volume. **Manganese dose-dependently induced astrocyte swelling; such swelling was first observed at 12 h (28%), which further increased (54%) at later time points (24–48 h). Pretreatment of astrocyte cultures with antioxidants, including vitamin E, the spin trapping agent PBN, and the iron-chelating agent desferroximine, as well as the nitric oxide synthase inhibitor l-NAME, all significantly blocked (50–80%) astrocyte swelling caused by manganese, suggesting that oxidative/nitrosative stress is involved in the mechanism of such swelling**. Cyclosporin A, an inhibitor of mPT also blocked (90%) manganese-induced astrocyte swelling. The data indicate that manganese exposure results in astrocyte swelling and such swelling, at least in part, may be caused by oxidative stress and/or mPT. Astrocyte swelling by manganese may represent an important aspect of manganese neurotoxicity, and may be a factor in low-grade brain edema associated with chronic hepatic encephalopathy.

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T22:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23994>

Quelsen there had to be a weather astro some event Saturday. I had to make myself get in a pool, for flips sakes. It felt like nothing...idk why I was avoiding it.

I have a neuro genesis tidbit ONE nights sleep deprivation, not more.

[http://www.biopsychiatry.com/neurogenesis-sleepdep.htm](http://http://www.biopsychiatry.com/neurogenesis-sleepdep.htm)

'One night' sleep deprivation stimulates hippocampal neurogenesis

ABSTRACT

Neurogenesis in the adult hippocampus can be up- or downregulated in response to a variety of physiological and pathological conditions. Among these, dysregulation of hippocampal neurogenesis has been recently implicated in the pathogenesis of depression. In addition, in animal models of depression, a variety of antidepressant treatments reverse that condition by increasing neurogenesis. **As one night sleep deprivation is known to improve mood in depressed patients for at least 1 day, we investigated **whether a comparable treatment may affect hippocampal neurogenesis in adult rats.** Accordingly, rats were sleep-deprived by gentle handling for 12h during their physiological period of rest, and were injected with bromodeoxyuridine 4h and 2h before the end of sleep deprivation. They were then perfused immediately thereafter, or after 15 days and 30 days. We found that 12h sleep deprivation significantly increased cell proliferation and the total number of surviving cells in the hippocampal dentate gyrus soon after sleep deprivation, as well as 15 days and 30 days later, in comparison to control rats allowed to sleep. No changes were instead found in the subventricular zone of the lateral ventricles, indicating that 12h sleep deprivation selectively triggers neurogenic signals to the hippocampus.** The present data include acute sleep deprivation among the conditions which upregulate hippocampal neurogenesis and raise the possibility that such response could be implicated in the beneficial effects elicited in depressed patients by one night sleep deprivation. Thus, the findings could contribute to the understanding of the intriguing relationship between depression and neurogenesis in the adult brain.

### MonteD — 2012-05-12T19:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23439>

I don't like cool sculpting. Its only approved for the love handles and it cost 1500 dollars per 1 hour session. And the guy in the video had a good 25 pounds to lose and was worrying about "love handles"....lol. With as much as he is going to spend on the doc (and subsequently gain back) he could have invested in a complete cryo setup at home.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T21:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23468>

But Monte what is very important here is that science is now aware that fat cells empty and go away with cold......right now they are using it for vanity.......I have been pounding the table for 7 yrs that this is the answer for health, obesity and diabetes.........The next leap is not that large from cool sculpting.......I saw that earlier than most......and it was into my CT 4 and CT 6 blogs. They are critical parts of the CT series.

So many people who view this cold issue from a narrow perspective call it hormetic.........It is not hormetic at all. It is curative for many patients with circadian mismatches. It maybe hormetic for a performance athlete who has a different goal.......but when I here a leader call cold a hormetic only thing........well that tells me they are pretty closed minded about how far reaching this science can be. I on the other hand have expanded this science to its outer boundaries in what I have been able to do and how I have demonstrated it to work in humans. How I am now using CT in neurosurgery is blowing this hormetic issue completely out of the window but only those with an open mind will see that because some "paleo leader" calls it hormesis only. When I have definitive proof that I can remove a tumor and do surgeries on people I normally could not do it on before it opens the question of what really is the boundary. A closed mind puts a frame or boundary around a concept based upon our current knowledge.

The great danger of success is thinking what made you successful will keep you successful........

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-13T01:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23537>

> **Jack Kruse;23550 wrote:** But Monte what is very important here is that science is now aware that fat cells empty and go away with cold......right now they are using it for vanity.......I have been pounding the table for 7 yrs that this is the answer for health, obesity and diabetes.........The next leap is not that large from cool sculpting.......I saw that earlier than most......and it was into my CT 4 and CT 6 blogs. They are critical parts of the CT series.
>

>

When I have definitive proof that I can remove a tumor and do surgeries on people I normally could not do it on before it opens the question of what really is the boundary. A closed mind puts a frame or boundary around a concept based upon our current knowledge.
>
>
>
> The great danger of success is thinking what made you successful will keep you successful........

Jack, I appreciate your last sentence. We had a saying in the fighter pilot community--You are only as good as your next sortie. What people consider success today in the medical community (and I would submit, in many others) is actually total failure when you look at it from a higher altitude. The medical community is making money hand over fist, but the people are just getting sicker. This is somewhat analogous to what has happened to the financial community--the banksters get richer, and the rest of the people get poorer and suffer. Whatever happened to the ethic of real service to your fellows and your communities? When you view both the medical community and the financial community as exemplars, why aren't they viewing the results of their professions from a higher level and actually see the obvious--they are just making things worse even if they may firmly believe they are doing the right thing. That's what CW will do to you. What made you successful, may, in the end, be the seeds of your own demise. You are only as good as your next sortie!!

### AmyK — 2012-05-13T02:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23547>

> **Randychaps;23619 wrote:** Jack, I appreciate your last sentence. We had a saying in the fighter pilot community--You are only as good as your next sortie. What people consider success today in the medical community (and I would submit, in many others) is actually total failure when you look at it from a higher altitude. The medical community is making money hand over fist, but the people are just getting sicker. This is somewhat analogous to what has happened to the financial community--the banksters get richer, and the rest of the people get poorer and suffer. Whatever happened to the ethic of real service to your fellows and your communities? When you view both the medical community and the financial community as exemplars, why aren't they viewing the results of their professions from a higher level and actually see the obvious--they are just making things worse even if they may firmly believe they are doing the right thing. That's what CW will do to you. What made you successful, may, in the end, be the seeds of your own demise. You are only as good as your next sortie!!

Love these thoughts, Randychaps! Many people in the medical community ARE in it to genuinely serve--not just for profit. Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-13T02:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23551>

> **AmyK;23629 wrote:** Love these thoughts, Randychaps! Many people in the medical community ARE in it to genuinely serve--not just for profit. Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

AmyK. Totally agree. Some individual responsibility needs to be taken. But what action to take needs to be informed. Unfortunately, the message out there, for example, the food pyramid, that is supposedly authoritative for an individual to take informed action, is little more than a consensus of opinion heavily influenced by lobbying from the food industry and contributions to various political campaigns and candidates. It is certainly not a strictly scientifically based food pyramid. So, individuals are responsible for their own health, they expect sound guidance from the "authorities", and yet act on less than enlightened so-called conventional wisdom. No wonder they get sick and don't know what to do about it. At least Dr. Kruse attacks the problem from a scientific perspective, that is, an evolutionary biology perspective, which is, in my mind, intuitively a more sound approach than a politically and a make-money based approach.

The system is clearly broken. So, if it is broken, you need to stop doing what you are doing and find another path. Dr. Kruse is doing just that. It is working for me. That's good enough for me. I hope it works for everybody!

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-13T02:54:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23554>

> **AmyK;23629 wrote:** Love these thoughts, Randychaps! Many people in the medical community ARE in it to genuinely serve--not just for profit. Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

Also, I know many people involved in the medical profession, and, to a person, they are hardworking, and want to help patients get healthy and stay healthy. But, they too must have the best informed information to take the appropriate action. So, again, it gets down to what "science" is being espoused and is it based on real evidence or some biased statistical correlation that under any reasonable scrutiny is easily dissected and declared as voodoo science. It is a total mess right now. But the great people working in the system are somewhat handicapped by bureaucracy, policy, protocol, and the threat of lawsuit. Very tough problem that will probably take a decade or two to correct--AT LEAST. But I will continue to do my own research, my own N=1 experiments, and make my own conclusions about what works for me and what doesn't. So far, Jack's Blog just resonated with me back last August. It was so integrative! I had tried just the Paleo diet (and even gave Crossfit a go) before and always got hung up at some point and could never break through to where I wanted to be. Now, integrating all kinds of scientific data, Jack has essentially come up with a fairly straightforward and actionable path to health. Eat a Paleo Diet, embrace chronic exposure to cold (CT), get good sleep, and minimize your mismatches with your environment, technology, and your biologically mandated circadian rhythms. Simple solution, not necessarily easy to implement, but worth a shot. I took the shot, there was no danger (Top Gun ), and I have seen remarkable benefits to my own health. And, I have convinced a few others to take the leap, give it a try, and see if it is or isn't effective. So far, all seems to be progressing just as Jack has laid it out. Can't ask for much more. Beats "you have high cholesterol; here is your prescription for statins." Really? Really.

### AmyK — 2012-05-13T03:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23562>

> **Randychaps;23633 wrote:
>
> The system is clearly broken. So, if it is broken, you need to stop doing what you are doing and find another path. Dr. Kruse is doing just that. It is working for me. That's good enough for me. I hope it works for everybody!

But, they too must have the best informed information to take the appropriate action. So, again, it gets down to what "science" is being espoused and is it based on real evidence or some biased statistical correlation that under any reasonable scrutiny is easily dissected and declared as voodoo science. It was so integrative! I had tried just the Paleo diet (and even gave Crossfit a go) before and always got hung up at some point and could never break through to where I wanted to be. Now, integrating all kinds of scientific data, Jack has essentially come up with a fairly straightforward and actionable path to health. Eat a Paleo Diet, embrace chronic exposure to cold (CT), get good sleep, and minimize your mismatches with your environment, technology, and your biologically mandated circadian rhythms. Simple solution, not necessarily easy to implement, but worth a shot. I took the shot, there was no danger (Top Gun ), and I have seen remarkable benefits to my own health. Dr. Kruse is taking his own path, for sure, and I have no doubt he has and will continue to help improve the lives of an infinite number of people and inspire other practitioners to do the same. I am on board. He is, however, still practicing medicine within and bound by the laws of the current, broken "system".

I totally agree about the ridiculousness of prescribing medications and not addressing the root cause. Statins are just the beginning...it frustrates me to no end! It is difficult, though, to consider actually returning to school for another healthcare degree, ignoring everything I will be taught, but ultimately being able to legally advise people.

Maybe it will be enough to teach yoga, send everyone to this site, and occasionally compound bioidentical hormones on the side...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-13T04:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23576>

The problem is that people out there think the solution is thru the literature and doing RCT's........and I don't any longer. Evolution did not need one for 4.5 billion yrs and neither do we. If we live as we should......we get better. Wild animals don't need colonoscopies and and mammograms because they live by circadian biology and eat a seasonal diet. Because we can color outside the lines........we need the current system. It is that simple.

### AmyK — 2012-05-13T15:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23641>

I posted this thought on the Factor-X thread, but I think it belongs as a response here as well: It is unfortunate that we MUST color outside the lines... and ironic that coloring outside the lines is what leads to the optimal specimen/work of art (i.e. David).

Perhaps there is more I can work with while still in my current position...

# Thread: Salivary Cortisol Test - how to do?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T18:12:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23404>

I'm planning to do a salivary cortisol test for myself (4 samples per day) and want to be sure I don't mess it up. The kit had good instructions but didn't answer all my questions. I know many of you have experience with this and hope you can comment on this please!

1) Should I avoid exercise the day of the test? Will a strength session in the gym or a easy-to-moderate bike ride interfere with cortisol production and thereby skew results?

2) The instructions say to freeze each vial after filling and then mail it in the pre-paid bag when I'm done. Is it worth upgrading return shipping to overnight, especially now that temperatures are warming? I don't want to go through the time & expense only to find questionable results.

3) The instructions about taking/continuing hormones are confusing. It says to stop sublingual hormones 36 hours before testing but in another place says not to stop taking hormones. The instructions appear to apply to all their saliva-based tests including hormone testing which I'm not doing so perhaps that advice doesn't apply?

4) Anything else I should know before doing the test? I will have stopped doing CT for a week before I do the test.

### PrimalPam — 2012-05-12T20:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23452>

I'm about to do salivary cortisol testing myself, and I have the kit from Canary. It also says to not wear any lip products at all on the day of testing (not even lip balm). Reading your question about upgrading to overnight shipping made me realize that I'm going to go ahead and do this as you said, due to warmer temps.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T20:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23453>

it measures a 6 -8 month window so what you do a few weeks before it does not matter. This is why it is a better test than a serum level

### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T20:42:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23457>

> **PrimalPam;23534 wrote:** I'm about to do salivary cortisol testing myself, and I have the kit from Canary. It also says to not wear any lip products at all on the day of testing (not even lip balm). Reading your question about upgrading to overnight shipping made me realize that I'm going to go ahead and do this as you said, due to warmer temps.

The no lip balm (I love my Lip Trip!) is going to be a bummer but oh well, a small sacrifice on the path to optimal.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T20:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23458>

> **Jack Kruse;23535 wrote:** it measures a 6 -8 month window so what you do a few weeks before it does not matter. This is why it is a better test than a serum level

Thanks! Very good to know.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T20:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23459>

I have the ZRT test and it is a little confusing with the hormones. Personally, I assumed it was meant with regard towards hormone testing. Something to ask the company about.

I've had this test sitting around for months now. I attempted twice to take it and each time AN threw a fit. My brain still cannot take "can't eat" for 4 hours, apparently. Reason enough to send it into overdrive and think that I must eat everything now. So I aborted the tests. Very strange, since I can not eat just fine according to leptin rx most days... the difference is I \*could\* snack, I just don't want to.

### freshveggies — 2012-05-12T21:30:25+0300

The instructions appear to apply to all their saliva-based tests including hormone testing which I'm not doing so perhaps that advice doesn't apply?

this site mentions supplements that can interefere and to be off of them for 2 weeks before testing. That will be hard for me. [http://www.adrenalsweb.org/meds-supplements.php](http://http://www.adrenalsweb.org/meds-supplements.php)

### nonchalant — 2012-07-07T00:20:22+0300

# Thread: Blog-Can Dr. Jack's responses to comments go directly under the comment itself?

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### PrimalPam — 2012-05-12T18:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23409>

Is there any way for Dr. Jack's responses to comments go directly under the comment to which he's referring? Rather than having to scroll back up to see which post he's referring to?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T18:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23420>

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-12T19:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23426>

### Lyndra — 2012-05-13T18:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23684>

I'd like to be able to read comments two ways: by date and threaded (replies indented just below comment). Good luck finding a way to make this work and keep everybody happy!

### admin — 2012-05-14T19:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23934>

We had that implemented a couple months ago and we got complaints that they wanted comments on the older platform. I do agree it would be easier to read with his responses directly under the initial comment. As Dr. Kruse mentioned, if enough people request it we'll re-implement it.

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T20:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23952>

# Thread: Finish strong!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 43
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-12T19:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23427>

I'm starting LR tomorrow, and here's SOME of my story.

I was never overweight until I was hit by a car on my bike in 2003, tore up my knee (terrible triad) which deteriorated over a 3yr period with two scopes until I finally had a total knee replacement in 2006 at Mayo (those surgeons rock!). I'm getting along great with the knee. Can do everything but squat (it doesn't want to bend quite that far). Prior to that I managed to keep at a decent weight although it was a constant challenge. I did all the same yo-yo's as everybody else.

I've been under a LOT of stress most of my adult life. I lost a a son when I was 34, divorce followed. Single mom for 17 yrs with a youngest son that turned to drinking and pot in high school, which led to arrest, OMVI's, jail, etc. that went on for years, still isn't completely under control but I've made it not my problem now. Just trying to give you a sense of my YEARS of high stress. Remarried ten years ago about the time I started menopause, turned out to be a jerk and that whole ten years was constant stress!! I'm now going through another divorce, which is a good thing, and CLEANING UP MY LIFE!!

After the accident in 2003 I gained 50 lbs over the next 3 yrs from being sedentary. Lost 40 two winters ago with hcg. About 3 months ago I started comfort eating and gained 15 pounds back. I'm right now close to 20 pounds over what I consider a healthy weight for me. I just finished another round of hcg to get that weight back off, and being on the website a lot led me to Jack Kruse and LR. (It's a God thing!) So....last several weeks I've been reading, reading, questioning, planning to do LR instead of hcg's P3.

I've read Taube's Why We Get Fat, and have started the Primal Blueprint. Bought The Paleo Solution last night. I have no problem with going against CW, so this is not a problem for me, and I'm completely fascinated by all of it! I have some heart disease (dad's side) and my mom died with dementia, so at 63 I'm looking to _live well and drop dead!_

I'm one of those "jumpy" people - like always stressed even when I think I'm not. This winter I did some Reflexology which was awesome! Definitely helped me feel more calm and loosen some muscles. Right now I'm seeing a Physical Therapist that does cranio sacral work, and that is helping my sleep and helping me feel calm also - in the face of going through a divorce and breaking up with my best friend (not my best friend after all). I work in a Physical Therapy clinic where we are trying to address all aspects of wellness, so I'm in a good place there. I believe it's time for a metamorphosis!

My goal is get healthy - hopefully that includes a healthy weight I can get to fairly soon. But more energy, better sleep, stronger bones and muscles, great attitude! It would be awesome if, in the next year or so, I become an example at my age of what this way of life can do for you. I'm taking before pictures

I haven't had all the labs done they talk about, but I know my cholesterol/trig numbers are awesome, and everything else I know about is good. Other than some mild atherosclerosis, I haven't been diagnosed with anything, so that's my base of knowledge!

So, tomorrow, on Mother's Day, I officially start LR, and will journal my progress here. I'm braced for ups and downs, but have high expectations!

Judge your successes by what you had to give up to get it! Dalai Lama

### Lanie — 2012-05-13T02:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23552>

Welcome, JustJoan, and good luck to you! Don't be afraid to ask questions - lots of very helpful people around here. You deserve to take care of you. Glad you're here to do it!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-13T05:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23591>

Thanks for the welcome, Lanie! I have asked Jack a couple of questions, and he's great about answering! I didn't really mean to start LR today, but it kind of turned out that way! I got up and had 4 eggs fried in CO and two Jimmy Dean sausage patties (I know, a little sugar in them), but I ended up at 42 g protien so not too bad, so I decided to take a stab at a first day. Had about a 6 oz ground beef patty fried in CO, steamed green beans then drizzled the left over oil in the pan over the beans. 8 oz ribeye and mixed salad for supper which turned out to be a little late. Time got away from me. I used a little EVOO with some Herbs de Provence and thyme in it for dressing. I'm drinking some water with lemon juice and Stevia in it this evening. Hope that's ok! Jack said if it's got calories it's a snack and this has no calories, so hope it's ok. If it's not, somebody let me know!!  Already seem to have more energy - dont know if it's in my head or not, but I'll take it! Happy Mother's Day to me!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-17T02:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24743>

Joan I answered your questions over on my journal.

On the hormone thing, if you're serious about it then you should find a Wiley Protocol [practitioner](http://http://www.wileyprotocol.com/wp-directory/find-a-wp-provider.html).

The HRT study your current doc referenced? According to TS Wiley, when the results were made public they conveniently "forgot" to mention that EVERY SINGLE WOMAN was on SYNTHETIC hormones. They might as well have been taking rat poison.

Actually, even horse estrogen provides a little bit of protection but when you add in synthetic progesterone it's like pouring gasoline on a fire. You may need to educate your doc and his p.a.

On the grass fed, be sure and ask if it's grass fed or grass finished. They're two different things. Don't let 'em fool ya.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-19T16:58:05+0300

I was down .5 lb this morning, so I think I've narrowed it down to intolerance to CO being the culprit. I still ate high protein fairly big breakfast, no lunch, some raw almonds and an oz of 85% chocolate in the afternoon, quite a bit of pork roast and some broccoli with butter for supper. Took my Golden for about a 2 mile walk around the lake, and retrieving in the water. Stayed up a little later than usual watching The Big Chill (love that movie!) and ate probably 2 more oz 85% chocolate.

So......I didn't sleep as well and woke up with a headache and feeling toxic. I also had some Physical Therapy on my knee which flared it up because it hurts worse this morning. I think I got something started then. My doctor at Mayo didn't think I have a meniscus tear.

4 eggs and 4 strips of bacon for breakfast plus coffee. Will try to get back to not snacking today. Also going out to try and find some K2 (Dr. recommended since I have some heart disease) and the mag that Dr. K recommends, or at least mag citrate to help keep things moving.

Am excited to see the BHRT doc this week. People who have seen her like her work. I'm anxious to finally see where my hormone levels are. I gave up on that going through menopause because nobody around here worked with it. But this got me on it again, and I found her! Puzzle pieces!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-20T22:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25812>

DAMN woman where'd you put all that breakfast?! LOL. I can do the eggs and the bacon but just barely.

You're probably tired because you aren't eating carbs. It WILL pass. Allow your body to adjust and it will eventually start burning fat. Eating coconut oil helps, but you already know that, right? My 2 cents ... DO NOT use safflower oil. If I understand it (and I may not have it right) vegetable oils are loaded with omega 6's. Except CO of course.

If the CO is causing you to bloat? Use less until you get used to it. You might try coconut butter (aka manna). Jack swears by Nutiva brand but my preference (guess I'm not THAT big a groupie after all, lol) is Artisana organic. It is YUMMMMMY. Pricey but yummy. Also the only coconut oil I will use is from Tropical Traditions. They're online. It's pricey but it's made by hand the old fashioned way (according to the website) and is so good I can eat it straight. It's not cheap. But a little goes a long way. I just got another gallon tub of it (which weighs about 10 pounds, I swear) so we're now good for several months.

I doubt this will help but after I did 18 months on that awful jenny diet and then 8-9 months on hcg all I did was gain. 20 on, 20 off, 20 back on with another 24 friends. I have given up worrying about it. That's the best advice I have. Let go. This is not a diet. This will take time for those of us with long-term serious issues. Every day that passes I become more convinced it will work and when the flab begins to go I think/hope it will go FAST. But it WILL take time. Acceptance of that little fact has come hard for me. But I finally have gotten there. Now I just need to really stick with the program.

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-15T05:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-31286>

Well, other than advice about Vit D, which I can see, I don't understand anything. Feeling pretty discouraged about managing this program. Apparently, being within the normal limits on the lab report doesn't necessarily mean anything. The kind people giving me advice are only confusing me more - assuming I know what they know. I'm eating probably 1200 calories a day paleo and very slowly still gaining weight. Very frustrating! I meet with my BHRT doc tomorrow to go over these tests, and hope she knows what she's talking about. I don't understand why she didn't test more hormones, I thought she was. When I first saw the results I thought they looked pretty good, but........now I don't know anything. I was really hoping that I'd found something great for me. But I can't go back on LR and gain a pound a day - won't do it. Living alone and being 63, I'm a little afraid to do CT in case of a problem. Please tell me I don't have to resign myself that menopausal woman means fat.....period. And, I saw the ortho surgeon today who thinks I have a meniscus tear in my good knee. Got sideswiped by dogs at the dog park a couple of times this winter - maybe happened then. Been there - done that - and it was a slippery slope after my first scope with the other knee. So right now I can't even go for a long walk with my dog. I know, poor me, pitty party. I've just invested so much time the last two months reading here, buying books, buying food, reading Primal Blueprint, Paleo Solution, trying to understand everything, not cheating. I can't afford numerous lab tests, so was hoping these would reveal something. Right now I'm just confused and discouraged.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-15T19:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-31380>

JustJoan, throw out the calorie counter.

What are you eating? Please post and don't bother with amounts other than the BAB.

Since I have been having the same problem I'm going to ask if you perhaps might maybe have food allergies you are not aware of?

Do you have a problem with constipation? and then unexplained runs?

Mouth tingling or itching?

Occasional trouble breathing?

Stomach hurting after you eat?

I have had all those symptoms and until the Great Mushroom Incident it never occurred to me I might have food allergies.

So far I have HAD to quit eating:

nuts

eggs

FISH

mushrooms

maybe chocolate

maybe chicken

when I don't I really pay for it.

I'm also drinking 8 oz kombucha daily alternating (when I have it) with kefir. Pickles, yes. Saurkraut, yes. Also taking probiotics all in an attempt to heal what is obviously a leaky gut.

Allergies, as I understand it (which doesn't mean much) are an inflammation which stems from a leaky gut (if I'm wrong I'm sure Jack will step in and set me straight). Inflammation makes you FAT.

Fix the inflammation, watch the pounds go away.

At least that's what I'm hoping.

HTH

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-16T02:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-31460>

Hi Darlene. I had my BHRT doc appt today and we talked about the possibility of food allergies. I have had a problem with constipation for years, but none of the other symptoms you named. I have almost completely quit dairy the past several weeks (which is HUGE for me because I love cheese!), except for some heavy cream now and then. We talked about quitting eggs and seeing what happens - that Suzanne Sommers in her book quit eggs and lost 10 lbs. That would be nice! I don't have any food allergies that I know of, but will try quitting eggs and see if anything happens.

We also talked about heavy metals, which got tested even though she didn't mean to. Her nurse apparently ordered the wrong labs, which really honks me off after getting stuck FIVE times trying to get blood!!! grrrr.... So we didn't get some things we thought we would, and did get some things she didn't mean to. Maybe just as well. I have some mercury (probably like everybody), but she said it can make you gain. We did get testosterone which is low. Since we didn't get all the hormones tested, she sent me home with a saliva test kit which I'm doing tomorrow. I'm also doing a urine thing for metals. AND, she went ahead and ordered some BHRT for me to get started based on the quite probable results, so I'm really glad about that.

I have been taking a probiotic, but am going to get a better quality one and more of it from the compounding pharmacist. She's trying to address the constipation with it. This new clinic is getting some machine to test metabolism (of which I'm pretty sure I have none!), so we'll do that eventually. She thinks I've got adrenal issues probably which I diagnosed myself with several years ago, but again the doctors get that "deer in the headlights" look when you ask about it in the past! Surely cortisol is a problem with going through a divorce, etc.

Back to your question, I've been eating eggs and bacon or just eggs for breakfast. (Not trying to do the reset right now until I get some hormones straightened out because I was gaining a pound a day. Eating more paleo-like.) I fix a mixed veggie salad usually for lunch with some olives, feta cheese, along with greens, cucumber, tomato, bell pepper, and have chicken or tuna. Usually beef or pork for supper with steamed cauliflower or whatever. I snack on almonds (no salt). I don't worry about nightshades because my HDL is good and that's what I understood Jack to say. I might snack on a square of baking chocolate dipped in Stevia. I'm not sure I do OK with almonds. I should cut them out and see what happens. I don't get any symptoms, but they might make me gain. I was only keeping track of what I was eating on Fitday to mainly keep track of my carbs because I've been trying to keep them pretty low to lose weight. If anything, I'm not sure I eat enough some days. It's pretty erratic, and maybe that confuses my body - don't know!

My D is low so I'm really ramping that up. Let me see....I think that's about it.

Thanks for your input. I'll let you know about the eggs.

What are the pickles, sauerkraut, etc. supposed to be doing for you? Does that balance stuff in the gut?

I feel better at least finally getting a start on things, with the BHRT and further testing!! I feel like I have confidence in her. She doesn't do insurance. You have to pay cash and file it yourself, so it's all getting a little expensive, but I feel like it's money well spent! I'll have a followup with her after the "spit" results are in!!

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-16T21:10:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-31589>

I'm doing the saliva test today. Holy crap! I have dry mouth from taking medications as it is.....took me half an hour to get enough saliva for the morning test. I smelled lemon juice, yawned, massaged my cheeks by my salivary glands. I managed to get enough for the second vial and have to do the third in a few minutes. I'm also this week doing chelation for metals in my body. Go big or go home!!! Want the doctor to have all the results by next time I go back.

I have some BHRT to start on - tomorrow - after I'm done with the saliva test. Also, no eggs this morning. I'm going to try eliminating them for awhile and see if it makes any difference in anything. I finally found a local source for grass fed/grass finished beef. The store was out but will have more in Tuesday - comes out of Missouri. We have such good beef here in Iowa. I hope more farmers will start feeding this way.

Oh, and I'm scheduled for an MRI Tuesday to confirm whether I have a meniscus tear. Then it's deciding whether to let the local guy scope it or go back to my knee replacement guy at Mayo. Oh, Dr. Kruse, I never read - did you eventually have your knee scoped and how is it feeling now? # Thread: Nutrition for Lymphoma/Leukemia?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Marie — 2012-05-13T06:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/post-23607>

Looking for suggestions for slowing the progression of Lymphoma and/or preparing for radiation treatment. Since they've been on a really healthy ketogenic diet for the last 7 years, I'm assuming herbs and increasing fat is the way to go.

Any suggestions or alternative treatments are greatly appreciated,

Marie

### hazyjane — 2012-05-16T23:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/post-24706>

One thing that would help them to handle the radiation better is Cell Food liquid oxygen. It was originally developed to offset radiation exposure.

Infrared saunas might be a treatment option (they are used in Europe as a cancer treatment along with the regular mainstream treatments). There is a form of qigong (a type of tai chi) widely used in China as a cancer treatment called "Guolin Qigong". You might be able to find a video teaching it. It's very simple to learn.

As far as herbs/supplements, turmeric and IP-6 would be worth researching.

### Adria — 2012-05-17T14:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/post-24847>

Marie, first I would assess the keto diet to be sure all natural, grass fed diet, no oils no fake foods, additives etc. Look at the water hes drinking, test it. I would suggest adding CT immediately. Sunshine in day and darkness at night. My DH was diagnosed with uncurable lymphoma @stage 4. He had monoclonal antibody treatment in a clinical trail and went into molecular remission. This was 17yrs ago! His dr said he had the longest remission in the US, for this trial. He eats keto-paleo, lifts weights, and CT. He still shows signs lymphoma the last 10yr but it is not aggressive.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-17T15:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/post-24857>

> **hazyjane;24788 wrote:** One thing that would help them to handle the radiation better is Cell Food liquid oxygen. It was originally developed to offset radiation exposure.
>
> Infrared saunas might be a treatment option (they are used in Europe as a cancer treatment along with the regular mainstream treatments). There is a form of qigong (a type of tai chi) widely used in China as a cancer treatment called "Guolin Qigong". You might be able to find a video teaching it. It's very simple to learn.
>
>
>
> As far as herbs/supplements, turmeric and IP-6 would be worth researching.

I agree with turmeric before during and after XRT......and IP=6 is a great CA tx adjuvant. There are others like NAC, milk thistle, fever few, vitamin D C E and A, Magnesium, CoEnQ10 and Selenium. I also think using fish oil and krill oil to block the conversion of DGLA to AA and limiting the inflammatory prostaglandins make a ton of sense. These are all things to talk to your oncologist and XRT people out. If they are not facile int his knowledge seek someone else out who is. Eating a low inflammatory diet is critical for cancer treatment in my view.

### Marie — 2012-05-18T00:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-for-lymphoma-leukemia.1432/post-25010>

> **Adria;24929 wrote:** Marie, first I would assess the keto diet to be sure all natural, grass fed diet, no oils no fake foods, additives etc. Look at the water hes drinking, test it. I would suggest adding CT immediately. Sunshine in day and darkness at night. My DH was diagnosed with uncurable lymphoma @stage 4. He had monoclonal antibody treatment in a clinical trail and went into molecular remission. This was 17yrs ago! His dr said he had the longest remission in the US, for this trial. He eats keto-paleo, lifts weights, and CT. He still shows signs lymphoma the last 10yr but it is not aggressive.

Thanks! This gives me (and him) hope!

His diet has been stellar, much better than mine!! It is just so weird because he seems to be doing everything right, so we've been looking at the fact that he was exposed to a lot of petroleum in the Gulf for about 3 years, prior to going keto, and wondering if that was what initiated it. Since his blood work was normal in Nov., we know this is a recent decline which could have been triggered by a REALLY stressful couple of weeks.

He has been living the woo woo lifestyle for +8 years, w/ quarterly acupuncture and chiro, lots of sleep (semi retired) and sunshine. The only thing he doesn't do is supplement anything more than Fish oils.

Thx!

Marie

# Thread: Epigenetics does not mean that thinking makes it so?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-does-not-mean-that-thinking-makes-it-so.1437/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MonteD — 2012-05-13T17:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-does-not-mean-that-thinking-makes-it-so.1437/post-23671>

> Yes, this is pure Secret-like wish fulfillment. Mercola totally buys into the idea that emotions can change epigenetics to the point where your emotional makeup can alter your gene expression. In a way, this is a rather trivial observation. Of course, if you'll probably change gene expression in parts of your brain because becoming angry or upset for example, raises blood pressure and produces a host of other physiological effects. Where Mercola and his fellow woo-meisters go off the rails on this crazy train is when they imply that you can control epigenetic processes with your thoughts and emotions. This is where Mercola's attacking the Central Dogma as a "myth" that can "give you cancer."
>
>
> Mercola's article is yet another supreme confirmation that magical thinking is at the heart of so much alternative medicine. No one argues that it's not a good thing to improve one's diet, to exercise, to avoid harmful substances. That's a no-brainer, and science can give us the parameters for what is harmful and what is not. Even if it is true that emotions change gene expression through epigenetic mechanisms, it does not follow from that that we can actually control our gene expression in a way meaningful enough to make a difference in health. As someone once said about Deepak Chopra when he made similar claims, I'd like to see Mercola or Lipton alter their gene methylation just by thinking about it.

From: [http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2012/04/epigenetics\_does\_not\_mean.php](http://http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2012/04/epigenetics_does_not_mean.php)

Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-13T17:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-does-not-mean-that-thinking-makes-it-so.1437/post-23673>

magical thinking is a Right brain endocrinologic secretion that affects the action of the cerebellum and left brain. It clearly changes the "switches on genes" because this is where creative thinking for exploration of the environment comes from. But when faced with survival creative thinking seems to be relegated to the best one thought. Ironically if you read survival data and books those who survive tragedy usually make their first decision their best.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-14T05:34:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-does-not-mean-that-thinking-makes-it-so.1437/post-23818>

Levee 14:

Epigenetic modifications is quick and dynamic.\* We saw in the PPAR gamma levee that dietary forces can force immediate gene transcription on the cell in question. Not only does diet induce immediate changes in DNA activation but so does the organism’s hormonal status.\*Moreover, humans are the only animals that can induce DNA changes just by the way we think.\*We know that DNA activation occurs with thoughts from functional MRI data and PET scans on brain function.\*Many call this area the mind/body connection but I find this to be a misnomer. The mind is the sum total function of the central nervous system (CNS) and its endocrine secretion is called a thought.\*That secretion can directly up regulate DNA and RNA activity with gene expression and protein formation.\*This happens within nanoseconds according to functional MRI data, and EEG strips.\*It’s nothing short of amazing to think evolution can move that fast on a micro level but still not affect the phenotype of the organism in any great macro way.\* Another paradox of life revealed I guess. (link)

### indigogirl — 2012-05-15T01:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-does-not-mean-that-thinking-makes-it-so.1437/post-24053>

[http://info.agscientific.com/blog/bid/117216/Epigenetics-reinforces-theory-that-positive-thinking-heals](http://http://info.agscientific.com/blog/bid/117216/Epigenetics-reinforces-theory-that-positive-thinking-heals)

Stole this from BK Davis on the Factor X thread!

The role of signaling pathways in the regulation of cellular iron metabolism is becoming increasingly recognized. Iron chelation is used for the treatment of iron overload but also as a potential strategy for cancer therapy, because i**ron depletion results in cell cycle arrest and apoptosis.**

grrr!

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-14T01:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-23764>

I had a doc 6 mo ago that wanted me to do infusions after I took supplements and had a bad reaction. He based his decision on my ferritin level (which is just the iron storage protein). When I talked to my husband about it he joked with me about iron oxidizing and do I really want to pump a bunch into my body... and asked me if how many more bottles of antioxidants we'd have to buy. He was joking around.... but it made me think.

So then a couple months ago life extension foundation put this out about Excess Iron and Brain Degeneration:

[http://www.lef.org/magazine/mag2012/mar2012\_Excess-Iron-Brain-Degeneration\_01.htm](http://http://www.lef.org/magazine/mag2012/mar2012_Excess-Iron-Brain-Degeneration_01.htm)

I've had some other blood tests done to evaluate my iron levels and have decided with another Dr. that I don't need supplementation. But it looks like iron is def. not something to take extra of unless one really has a problem.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-14T01:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-23772>

For the last 30+ years ever since I've been buying supplements I have always bought an iron free multivitamin. I was counseled early on by an alternative, orthomolecular MD not to take extra iron but can't remember why.

Anyone know?

### AmyK — 2012-05-16T04:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-24485>

Cavemam, the surgery performed on the patient I was thinking of did not involve bowel resection, so... unfortunately, I do not know of anyone who has any personal experience with iron injections or infusions after all. I haven't read the full text for that article, but, depending on how Factor-X ties into this, I may spend a few bucks to get it.

indigogirl, in case you did not yet receive an answer to your question… unless a person is found to be deficient, it's generally not recommended that they take supplemental iron. Our bodies have evolved an iron storage/recycling mechanism, probably to ensure survival in the event of rapid, severe blood loss. This is rarely a problem when getting iron from food, but taking supplements--especially if one has a genetic condition predisposing them to the accumulation of excess iron (oftentimes undiagnosed)--can have many severe consequences. See the article posted above by RiverNeighbor and [http://www.irondisorders.org/iron-overload](http://http://www.irondisorders.org/iron-overload)

### indigogirl — 2012-05-16T06:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-24516>

Thanks for the info Amy.

### AmyK — 2012-05-16T17:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-24591>

> **indigogirl;24598 wrote:** Thanks for the info Amy.

You're welcome.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-11-15T18:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-64516>

### MsYo — 2012-11-15T19:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-64536>

Mine is increasing. I was SSS Tonic (liquid) but recently switched to UNILIVER tabs (recommended by JanZ)and the improvements can be felt after 4 weeks of use. I won't retest until early 2013 but I can tell by my breathing during small stints of cardio. Before I would feel as if I would collapse, now it is VERY different (improved).

### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-16T05:43:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-64655>

Iron withholding by creation of transferrin is normal to keep bad bacteria from proliferating. It is normally simple for the body to access the ferritin stores. However, if the levels of hepcidin made by the liver are too high because of inflammatory cytokines then iron withholding can result in 'anemia of chronic disease' in which not enough red blood cells can be made.

### Glamazon — 2012-11-16T07:58:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-64674>

A few years ago I was diagnosed with chronic anemia with a ferritin level of 3. It was a complication of a surgery I had in 2001 that left malabsorption issues. Regular oral supplementation was tried but it was too slow. I ended up having to take 3 IV bags of iron, one bag a month for 3 months. I felt like superman for a week after. It was the first big hint of what feeling healthy was all about. Dr. K and I discussed that in a consult why that was so but I forgot to record and write down what he said about that.

I still have to take daily supplementation and if I slack off my blood work shows it.

### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-17T01:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-iron.1438/post-64863>

The more Hepcidin the liver makes the less iron the body absorbs from food? I read that inflammatory cytokines can cause an increase in hepcidin resulting in anemia.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-the-final-frontier-i-need-to-talk.1449/post-23858>

Lately I've been waking up at 3:15 a.m. and staying awake till dawn, then drifting off for another hour or so....so the last couple of days I have been doing open studios (art show) with friends, and while I didn't eat a single incredible homemade choc. chip cookie, I did succumb to a few corn chips with salsa. I didn't drink the lemonade, but had some wine. I'm trying to say, in a tight spot I may not be perfect but I'm nowhere near SAD. (Eating chips is actually a little too close for comfort)....

Thanks for listening.

### freesia — 2012-05-14T10:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-the-final-frontier-i-need-to-talk.1449/post-23860>

Night waking like that makes things very hard.  You have my sympathy, and understanding.

I've been waking up at approx 3am too - every night for the past week. Same as you I get back to sleep much, much later. It's tough getting up for that 6.30am alarm clock.

I think, in my case at least, toxin/hormone dump is stressing adrenals more. i don't notice any negative through the day (thank you LR!) but nights are a write-off currently!

And yeah it can make you more vulnerable to reaching for pick-me-ups during the day. I've had 2 cups of coffee with my BAB 3 days out of the last 4.

Hang in there. Just think how great the next full night's sleep is going to feel. Just like when a baby finally decides to sleep right through the night!
