# Thread: The Man's Thread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2012-05-16T19:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-24617>

A place to discuss male problems, not problems with males.

I'll go 1st.

About 3 or 4 years ago, I was using testosterone cream for low testosterone. After several months of usage, my PSA jumped up to 14.4. My doc sent me to a urologist who put me on an antibiotic (cipro) for 30 days. When my PSA was retested, it had dropped back to normal. So evidently I had a UTI that caused the PSA to spike.

Fast forward to now. I have been taking 7-keto for about a year, pregnenolone for about 5 months, and dhea for about a month. My PSA jumped up to 6.6 and DHEA went off the scale. At the same time, I started having severe urinary problems and a week later had blood in urine. Doc prescribed a sulfur drug and blood stopped after 1st pill. After about 2 weeks on drug, urinary problems are almost gone and dhea has returned to normal. Will retest PSA when I finish the pills.

So I put 2 and 2 together and think I know the answer. The question is, _Can supplementing with dhea and pregnenolone cause PSA to go up and cause urinary problems?_ Based on my experience, I think yes.

I did some research and here's what I found:

> **DHEA and BPH
>
>
>
> Studies have centered around the role of testosterone and dihydrotestosterone, or DHT on the prostate cell proliferation, or growth. A 2006 study reported in the American Journal of Physiology - Endocrinology and Metabolism, indicated that DHT and testosterone promote prostate growth, but that DHEA itself had no direct effect.
>
> Considerations
>
>
>
> Although some studies have not found DHEA culpable in stimulating prostate cell proliferation, **it remains the jump off point for production of sex hormones that do promote the growth of prostate cells.** As such, you may want to consult your doctor if you have concerns about BPH and an interest in DHEA supplements
>
> **

**
Read more: [http://www.livestrong.com/article/265049-bph-and-dhea/#ixzz1v34Akrkg](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/265049-bph-and-dhea/#ixzz1v34Akrkg)

Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH)

**

Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) also known as Benign prostatic hypertrophy or Benign enlargement of the prostate (BEP) refers to the increase in size of the prostate in middle-aged and elderly men. To be accurate, the process is one of hyperplasia rather than hypertrophy, but the nomenclature is often interchangeable, even amongst urologists. In BPH, the prostate grows larger and presses against the urethra and bladder, interfering with the normal flow of urine. It leads to symptoms of urinary hesitancy, frequent urination, increased risk of urinary tract infections and urinary retention. There is little correlation between BPH symptoms and the presence of prostate cancer.

Symptoms

Urinary symptoms of hesitancy, sensation of incomplete voiding and frequently passing small amounts of urine are all suggestive of BPH in middle-aged and elderly men. Due to the incomplete voiding, there is stasis of bacteria in the bladder residue and an increased risk of urinary tract infections.

Incomplete voiding (emptying) of the bladder caused by BPH also provides suitable conditions for the development of urinary bladder stones. These are formed from the crystallization of waste salts in the urine. Symptoms of bladder stone include blood in the urine and moderate to severe pain which can feel as though it is located in the penis, or around the anus, as well as the bladder. As the stone (which is often spikey) develops in size it produces increased pain and gradual reduction in mobility.

A small proportion presents with urinary retention, in which inadequate amounts of urine are passed and the bladder distends greatly. Untreated, this leads to a decrease in renal function and hydronephrosis (obstructive uropathy).

Etiology

Androgens (testosterone and related hormones) are considered to play a permissive role in BPH by most experts. This means that androgens have to be present for BPH to occur, but do not necessarily directly cause the condition. This is supported by the fact that castrated boys do not develop BPH when they age, unlike intact men. Additionally, administering exogenous testosterone is not associated with a significant increase in the risk of BPH symptoms. Dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a metabolite of testosterone is a critical mediator of prostatic growth. DHT is synthesized in the prostate from circulating testosterone.

DHT is localized principally in the stromal cells. Once synthesized, DHT can act in an autocrine fashion on the stromal cells or in paracrine fashion by diffusing into nearby epithelial cells. In both of these cell types, DHT binds to nuclear androgen receptors and signals the transcription of growth factors that are mitogenic to the epithelial and stromal cells. The importance of DHT in causing nodular hyperplasia is supported by clinical observations in which an inhibitor of 5?-reductase is given to men with this condition. Therapy with 5?-reductase inhibitor markedly reduces the DHT content of the prostate and in turn reduces prostate volume and, in many cases, BPH symptoms.

Read more at [http://www.consumersresearchcncl.org/Healthcare/Urologists/urologist\_chapters.html](http://http://www.consumersresearchcncl.org/Healthcare/Urologists/urologist_chapters.html)

So it appears that taking dhea, pregnenolone, or testosterone can cause an enlargement of the prostate and the problems that go with it. I don't see any choice but to stop taking these supplements.

So what can I do to increase testosterone naturally without taking either testosoterone or a precursor?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-16T19:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-24622>

> **huck;24699 wrote:** a place to discuss male problems, not problems with males.

lol!!

.......well, since I'm female I don't really got anything to offer

### JC2K — 2012-05-16T20:26:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-24645>

What are your current Free Testosterone and Estradiol levels at now? I'm assuming your Free Testosterone AND your Estradiol were both low originally (due to age), since you were getting the Testosterone cream and taking pregnenolone.

My side's a bit different, low Free Testosterone and high Estradiol (both within range though), probably due to bodyfat. So not so much a need for replacement Testosterone as a "reduce the conversion of Testosterone to Estradiol" sort of thing.

### Huck — 2012-05-16T20:40:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-24651>

> **JC2K;24727 wrote:** What are your current Free Testosterone and Estradiol levels at now? I'm assuming your Free Testosterone AND your Estradiol were both low originally (due to age), since you were getting the Testosterone cream and taking pregnenolone.
>
>
>
> My side's a bit different, low Free Testosterone and high Estradiol (both within range though), probably due to bodyfat. So not so much a need for replacement Testosterone as a "reduce the conversion of Testosterone to Estradiol" sort of thing.

Both are usually low. At the time my DHEA and PSA went high (2nd time), free testosterone direct was mid-range. Dr did not have any other labs run when I had high PSA the 1st time. Free testosterone is usually in lowest quartile of range or slightly below range.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-05-18T08:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-25133>

I'm not sure if this is helpful, but I just finished listening to a Sean Croxton podcast with TS Wiley (the second one he did, entitled Ask TS Wiley) and she talked about how testosterone levels cycle throughout the year, according to season, but CW docs know nothing of this. She said testosterone is lowest in winter, then increases in spring, goes very high in summer, and stays high in fall. She said that if you supplement with the same amount of testosterone year-round you will quickly turn off your receptors (I think that's the word she used) and end up with a problem again. If this sounds helpful, give the podcast a listen (it's from Sean Croxton's Underground Wellness radio, a great show!).

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-18T17:55:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-25193>

> **Huck;24699 wrote:** A place to discuss male problems, not problems with males.
>
>
>
> I'll go 1st.
>
>
>
> About 3 or 4 years ago, I was using testosterone cream for low testosterone. After several months of usage, my PSA jumped up to 14.4. My doc sent me to a urologist who put me on an antibiotic (cipro) for 30 days. When my PSA was retested, it had dropped back to normal. So evidently I had a UTI that caused the PSA to spike.
>
>
>
> Fast forward to now. I have been taking 7-keto for about a year, pregnenolone for about 5 months, and dhea for about a month. My PSA jumped up to 6.6 and DHEA went off the scale. At the same time, I started having severe urinary problems and a week later had blood in urine. Doc prescribed a sulfur drug and blood stopped after 1st pill. After about 2 weeks on drug, urinary problems are almost gone and dhea has returned to normal. Will retest PSA when I finish the pills.
>
>
>
> So I put 2 and 2 together and think I know the answer. The question is, _Can supplementing with dhea and pregnenolone cause PSA to go up and cause urinary problems?_ Based on my experience, I think yes.
>
>
>
> I did some research and here's what I found:
>
>
>
>
>
> Read more: [http://www.livestrong.com/article/265049-bph-and-dhea/#ixzz1v34Akrkg](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/265049-bph-and-dhea/#ixzz1v34Akrkg)
>
>
>
>
>
> Read more at [http://www.consumersresearchcncl.org/Healthcare/Urologists/urologist\_chapters.html](http://http://www.consumersresearchcncl.org/Healthcare/Urologists/urologist_chapters.html)
>
>
>
> So it appears that taking dhea, pregnenolone, or testosterone can cause an enlargement of the prostate and the problems that go with it. I don't see any choice but to stop taking these supplements.
>
>
>
> So what can I do to increase testosterone naturally without taking either testosoterone or a precursor?

OK......surface cream testosterone raises DHT and E2 more so than the shots.....a fact that is lost on most people. Two, if you have BPH changing your diet should be done. BPH is a disease tied to inflammation especially from the conversion DGLA to AA all stimulated by insulin!!!! This leads excessive leukotrienes and the inflammatory prostaglandins in excess in the prostate. PSA is a terrible marker for anything prostate these days so I am not sure why many still use it. The Urology literature states that with some power since 2011 meta analysis on it......If you have this issue you can stimulate T using HCG from the pituitaty by increasing LH. The issue is that LH surges stimulate the Testes to make Testosterone.......if your testes are no longer working well.....then HCG wont work. Most people default to Testosterone replacement.......it has its own set of issues so there are tradeoffs but the trade offs are small for most men who need it in my experience.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-05-18T18:40:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-25213>

> **KiwiLauren;25215 wrote:** I'm not sure if this is helpful, but I just finished listening to a Sean Croxton podcast with TS Wiley (the second one he did, entitled Ask TS Wiley) and she talked about how testosterone levels cycle throughout the year, according to season, but CW docs know nothing of this.

I think she co-wrote the book "Lights Out" - one of my old time favorites. it was the first time I started thinking about circadian rhythms.

### Huck — 2012-05-23T15:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mans-thread.1502/post-26651>

> **Jack Kruse;25275 wrote:** OK......surface cream testosterone raises DHT and E2 more so than the shots.....a fact that is lost on most people. Two, if you have BPH changing your diet should be done. BPH is a disease tied to inflammation especially from the conversion DGLA to AA all stimulated by insulin!!!! This leads excessive leukotrienes and the inflammatory prostaglandins in excess in the prostate. PSA is a terrible marker for anything prostate these days so I am not sure why many still use it. The Urology literature states that with some power since 2011 meta analysis on it......If you have this issue you can stimulate T using HCG from the pituitaty by increasing LH. The issue is that LH surges stimulate the Testes to make Testosterone.......if your testes are no longer working well.....then HCG wont work. Most people default to Testosterone replacement.......it has its own set of issues so there are tradeoffs but the trade offs are small for most men who need it in my experience.
>
>
> Jack, thanks for info. What do you think about using PSA and % Free PSA as an indicator? My PSA was 6.6 but % Free PSA was high indicating it was most likely not cancer causing the high PSA.
>
>
>
> Rather than subject everyone with an elevated PSA to a biopsy, some urologists measure free PSA in patients with a total PSA level between 4 ng/ml and 10 ng/ml. Studies have shown that men with a total PSA in this “gray area” and a free PSA greater than 25% are more likely to have a benign condition than to have cancer, making a biopsy unnecessary. Men with a total PSA in the same range and a free PSA below 10% need to have a biopsy. More likely than not, they have prostate cancer (see table below).

[http://www.harvardprostateknowledge.org/what-is-the-difference-between-psa-and-free-psa](http://http://www.harvardprostateknowledge.org/what-is-the-difference-between-psa-and-free-psa)

# Thread: My Functional Med Appt
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-05-16T22:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/post-24681>

Well, I met with the function med doctor and I guess I found it a little anti-climatic. She was very thorough and had this device that did Bioelectric Impedance Analysis. Basically its for cell membrane health. She said mine Phase angle was 5.9, which according to her chart means my cells are 62 years old  I am 40. I guess you can see why CT really sucked for me. My cell membranes are not in good shape.

It also said my BMI is 23.3 and I have a Fat Free Mass of 70.2% and Fat Mass of 29.8%. I am not happy with these numbers. She says my BMI is fine but I should try to build up some muscle and reduce fat mass. She's ordered a bunch of bloodwork, which I will have to find a doc in my insurance plan to write so it gets paid for. So that might take me a minute. She did suggest a new PMD, who is "with" functional medicine stuff. I probably will switch. Why wouldn't I? My doctor laughed at me when I told him I thought going gluten free had affected my thyroid.

So, bottom line is she says I have a leaky gut. Well, I knew that. I have many other things as well. She feels healing the leaky gut is primary and the other things will fall into place. So, I am on board with that, however we disagree about a few things. Primarily she wants me to do an elimination diet for 28 days. Well, there are a lot of things on an elimination diet that are NOT on a Paleo diet (brown rice, oats, quinoa, buckwheat, split peas, lentils, legumes, and all nuts except peanuts). There are major things on my Paleo diet that are NOT on the elimination diet, namely beef, pork, shellfish, ghee, chocolate. So, I am super unhappy about this...living off of seafood and foul. And she wants me to take this product called Ultra Clear Sustain. It has 10 g of sugar a serving and is made with rice flour and rice syrup. I do NOT want to take this. It's marketed for leaky gut and I cut the info and pasted at the bottom here. I am going to do some research and call her and say I don't want to take it. It also has Fructooligosaccharides and Inulin in it. I thought this was on the avoid list. She also recommended the three meals, two snacks stuff for low blood sugar. So, I am disappointed that she is not Paleo and IDK, seems like the GAPS diet would make more sense but this is what she feels is best course. I am going on vacation next week, so she said start it after. So I guess I have some time to think about this but my first reaction is I'm not sure how to do Leptin Rx and what she wants me to do.

lend, shake, or briskly stir about 2 level scoops (60 grams) of UltraClear SUSTAIN into 8 ounces of chilled water or juice twice daily or as directed by your healthcare practitioner. For highly sensitive individuals, consider reducing consumption to 1/2 to 1 scoop with 4 to 5 ounces of chilled water or juice twice daily during the first 3 to 4 days before starting a full daily dose.

Serving Size: About 2 Scoops (60 g)

Calories220 Fat4 gSaturated Fat†1.5 gTrans Fat gCholesterol mgSodium20 mgPotassium300 mgCarbohydrate32 gDietary Fiber3 gSugars10 gProtein15 gVitamin A (retinyl palmitate)1500 IUVitamin A (beta-carotene)2500 IUVitamin C102 mgCalcium300 mgIron3.6 mgVitamin D50 IUVitamin E50 IUThiamin0.525 mgRiboflavin0.595 mgNiacin7 mgVitamin B60.7 mgFolic acid140 mcgVitamin B122.1 mcgBiotin105 mcgPantothenic Acid100 mgPhosphorus175 mgMagnesium121 mgZinc7.5 mgCopper0.5 mgManganese1.3 mgChromium75 mcgL-Glutamine500 mgL-Lysine35 mgL-Threonine34 mgL-Cysteine25 mgL-Glycine100 mgDL-Methionine100 mgBlend of: Fructooligosaccharides and Inulin3 g

Other Ingredients: Rice protein concentrate

, rice syrup solids, rice flour, a blend of fructooligosaccharides and inulin, olive oil, magnesium citrate, calcium citrate, medium-chain triglycerides

, natural flavors, dicalcium phosphate, silica, D-calcium pantothenate, dipotassium phosphate, ascorbic acid, d-alpha tocopheryl acetate, zinc gluconate, L-lysine HCl, L-cysteine HCl, ferrous fumarate, vitamin A (beta-carotene, retinyl palmitate), niacinamide, copper gluconate, pyridoxine HCl

, thiamin HCl, chromium polynicotinate (as ChromeMateÂ® GTF††), cyanocobalamin, cholecalciferol

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-16T23:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/post-24695>

BIA are notorious not accurate but helpful for a trend.......the best use of it is to follow a phase angle decrease as you get healthier and you can use BIA for body comp measures between Dexa's because there is no radiation

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-16T23:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/post-24697>

Just eat your way and let her take care of the rest.......And if you want to change your cell membranes fast......ask her about the PKT protocol of PAtricia Kane. Most functional docs know about it. It can change things quick. Also Meyer's cocktail's for your gut

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-17T02:37:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/post-24741>

I hadn't heard of Myer's Cocktail for leaky gut so I looked it up:

A popular IV is the so-called Myers Cocktail, an intravenous vitamin and mineral protocol developed by Dr. John Myers at Johns Hopkins University in Maryland. He saw that many conditions stem from digestive problems – leaky gut, indigestion, bloating, and food sensitivities for example. He saw that these people were not absorbing many of the nutrients in their food. Also, some diseases cause the body to use nutrients at a faster rate, or to require higher amounts for proper healing. When nutrients are injected intravenously, we by-pass the problematic digestive tract. Levels in the bloodstream temporarily rise so that the nutrients are "coaxed" into the cells, and frequently into the mitochondria where they are active. This temporary boost frequently "kick-starts" the cells, so that energy is produced more efficiently in them.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-17T05:48:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-functional-med-appt.1506/post-24806>

> **Jack Kruse;24777 wrote: BIA are notorious not accurate but helpful for a trend.......the best use of it is to follow a phase angle decrease as you get healthier and you can use BIA for body comp measures between Dexa's because there is no radiation
>
>
> Jack Kruse;24779 wrote:** Just eat your way and let her take care of the rest.......And if you want to change your cell membranes fast......ask her about the PKT protocol of PAtricia Kane. Most functional docs know about it. It can change things quick. Also Meyer's cocktail's for your gut

Thanks Dr K! I'm glad to hear that I'm not really 62 yet! I am extremely interested in this how cell membrane stuff though, especially with a family history of Alzeheimer's Disease and Parkinson's Disease. (not to mention the diabetes) With her plan, I'm glad to have something concrete to focus on and of course, I'm sure the labs will show if anything else needs addressed.

A Meyer's cocktail sound great! (Thanks for looking it up, Patty!!) Good lord knows I'm not drinking any other cocktails these days! Now if only they were served somewhere in town! But I will write these two things down for my follow up appointment.

Now that I have had a little more time to process it, I will say this about her - she was very kind and understanding and she did a great explanation of leaky gut. She also said that as long as your gut is leaky, your body is at war and your adrenals are fighting it. So, even if you can get your outside stress down to zero, your adrenals will be taxed by the inner stress going on. She also gave a good explanation for the muscle fatigue. Basically saying that when the gut is leaking, then the liver is also overworked and things start backing up giving you the muscle fatigue. I know she is recommending the 5 meals because I complain about low blood sugar so loudly. But I am committed to Leptin Reset. This appointment confirmed it for me because I was totally freaking out inside when she was giving me the diet, etc. I can only imagine the look on my face. My adrenals were certainly taxed by that. The other thing I liked about her was that she only "prescribed" three things - Metagenics Ultra Flora Plus, Metagenics EPA-DHA 720 and the medical food that I don't want to take. She also recommended an adaptagin (Adreset) because I complained of my short temper and mood but I haven't done well on them, so I tabled it for now. Overall I just want to feel good again and not be such a short tempered byotch.

Sidebar...I was at an event tonight to announce the winning architect who is going to be building the new Medical School in downtown Buffalo. They had a list of all the areas they want to pursue teaching in...neurology, pediatric neurology, nephrology (esp kidney transplants) and all these other disease-focused areas. None were wellness focused and all I could think was, these are all probably caused by leaky gut and no one is doing anything about that. sigh...

# Thread: Webinars 101

### admin — 2012-05-16T23:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinars-101.1507/post-24709>

Hello all!

There have been a lot of questions and confusion about the webinar so we just wanted to clarify.

If you are a Kruse Karnivore or a member of the Kruse Klub then you will receive an e-mail about the webinar with directions.

If you pay for the webinar separately, then you should immediately be redirected to register. It is important that you register the day that you pay. If you do not register your seat is paid for, but not saved. Registeration guarantees your seat in the webinar. If you fail to register and the webinar sells out, then there is nothing we can do except refund your money.

As the date for the webinar approaches, you will receive a link to the webinar. When it is time-- all you have to do is click the link and it will take you to it. If you click the link before, it will tell you that it isn't time yet.

If it is your first time using gotowebinar.com, it will ask you to download something and then redirect you to the webinar.

If there are anymore questions or confusion please let us know so we can assist you.

Have an optimal day!

Jack's Support Team

\-JMM

### MJ* — 2012-05-17T03:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-24756>

Hello CT Friends,

Something very serious has come to my attention, and I hope to nip this in the bud for the sake of a man's excellent reputation. A man who has very selflessly done a lot for all of us. I have been made aware that Dr. Kruse's Facebook page has been hacked! I read this written by Doctor Kruse, and saw the pic he posted showing someone was in his computer.:

**Someone using a windows machine and firefox browser was using my FB account last week. I just changed all settings........notified FB and the authorities. I am sorry if this caused any problems but I am not totally aware of what has been posted since May 4th it appears.

**

These people are threatened by Dr. Kruse because his work raises questions for them about what they do. Clearly this is something that I would not put past a person or group, who found a way to make so much trouble for him that he was taken off the Low-Carb cruise. They are using their hack to try and spread more lies and make more trouble, PLEASE don't believe the crap you might see on some of these Paleo sites about Dr. Kruse. The trolls are just at it again...

MJ

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-17T14:09:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-24844>

### Optimalbound — 2012-05-17T14:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-24845>

If you have been using your computer on public wi-fi spots, it is relatively straightforward for a someone else to pick up your passwords.

I am disappointed by how quick many in the "paleo community" are to jump to conclusions based on what seems to be fairly scarce evidence and perform character assassination just because they disagree on the science or the way it is presented. Whether or not Dr. Kruse is 100% correct about everything (who is?) does not take away from the fact that he has brought a lot to the table and that many has benefited greatly from his generous sharing of his time and insights.

By the way, should jumping to conclusions be done in a fasted state?

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-05-17T14:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-24848>

I don't remember who called it free-the-jerk before, but I agree completely. I was subscribed to his feed something like two weeks last year. I have only come back because of Dr. Kruse's links (and this one too).

I keep thinking that Paul Jaminet would be a quite more interesting mind to bring about CT.

By the way, I am still subscribed to Melisa's and Dr. Deans' feeds. They uncover interesting information from time to time. I keep Dr. Harris' one too: it doesn't waste too much bandwidth . I simply ignore their opinion about Dr. Kruse. I am grown up enough to make my own judgment.

Well, it seems that Dr. Kruse has got to the second step of Mahatma Gandhi, perhaps beginning on the third: _First they ignore you, then they laugh at you, then they fight you, then you win._

I am sorry for so many posts, but Jack...you are way to naive and it's why all this is happening to you. I think you are honest--and for some reason people who spot this know how to manipulate this type of conscientiousness . I feel people can twist honesty when they have motive to discredit a work in progress and do so with little effort, for the reasons we believe lies over common sense. Common sense not being a possession of the masses, I am afraid to say. Not only that but don't think for one minute the AMA is not gonna go after you. They will go after you on principle, not because you are wrong. You take away from them and they will go after you BIG time. You are not the only pioneer this has happened to and you are not going to be the last. But I do see you as a bit naive (green).

You have what most people do not and that is your honesty. Don't let them discourage you, but keep on keeping on with caution. I for one will stand behind you even though I have no clout.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-24933>

@ Cavemam and anyone else that will listen. I find this disturbing because I have finally, after many of years of trying to be free from allopathic medicine, found all the pieces put together for optimal health. I was not aware that my use of homeopathy needed my help with the diet and supplements i was on. I wanted a magical cure like everyone else even though I didn't use allopathic medicine. Now I know that the responsibility is mine, HOWEVER, it was Mr. Dr. Jack Kruse that actually put all the pieces together. Forget that all these ideas are not original, but it was he who put them together and made it work by his own example.

I can't help it...I am disappointed in those who make sense much of the time, but cannot see their nose despite their face. I mean this is what I believe Jack means when he says that most people only strive for mediocre (the masses), but what is ultimately clear is these people miss the mark when it comes to seeing the big picture. There are few, like us, who see the big picture, who strive for excellence when it comes to who they are and what they accept as TRUTH.

Now I just think I'm angry but not at myself for believing Jack.

I hate it when others want to act like second class protoplasm. Dr. K's approach works for me, and that says it all.

I agree this is a great group of people, but darn leveled playing field, where's the fun in that? As for my profession...LoL...well...you've been schooled by a kindergarten teacher...I am a taxi mom these days...

I am seriously very impressed by everyone here as well, love reading all the ideas and contributions...I hope we keep learning together...

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-19T05:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25411>

> **Jack Kruse;25466 wrote:** Have you been thrown into a storm in life? Once the storm is over, you wonâ€™t remember how you made it through, how you managed to survive. You wonâ€™t even be sure, whether the storm is really over. But one thing is certain. When you come out of the storm, you wonâ€™t be the same person who walked in. Thatâ€™s what this stormâ€™s all about. Live your life your way.......if they dont like it, good. You are the author of your book. Write it your way.

I think we are mostly shaped by our Black Swan events. Average does not change us. Without change we do not grow. Growth comes from our experiences, and we get wisdom from our experiences especially the bad ones. But sometimes we think a Black Swan event is bad, yet we can use it to grow into a person that is way better than before. Bad begets good, good begets bad, birth begets death, and we call it life! And life is unpredictable, so enjoy it, whether high or low.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-19T11:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25455>

LOL, Ruby, so it seems, our own Dr. Kruse included. I mean, do humble people make blogs where they can share their own opinions with the world? Sometimes, but not often.

It's been amusing reading about bongo59 (yes, Dr. Kruse, we know it's you), but seriously, they're making a case that because he's a big-headed football lover who doesn't want his real name to get out, and who has at least as many people loving his football posts as hating them, that he's also a pathological liar? (How many of us have ever tried to stay anonymous on the internet? Is this really an expose? Please.)

And because Jack didn't give Richard \*all\* the details of his CT at the time of the phone call, that means he's a liar? How many of us (Richard, Chloe, Evelyn, and all the rest of the gang included) have ever withheld information while making what we did tell true, for what we believed was a good reason?

Stop me if I'm wrong, but does anyone remember Jack claiming to have done sub-freezing CT at all prior to this alleged post, much less for 8 months almost two years ago?

And let's imagine he IS a pathological exaggerator and/or liar ... does that change the facts he's spewing about health? I've checked them ALL (the ones that have been relevant to me), in numerous external sources, and not found one to be wrong yet. (Finding out he's a pathological maniac would certainly change my approach and use of his website, but it wouldn't discredit the information itself. And no, claiming his inside info on Phelps is a lie without proof to the contrary doesn't prove you right, nor does "proving" he's "lying" about the FBI by citing ... Melissa.)

This has never been about Jack Kruse for me. He's a generous guy with a big head giving me a lot of guidance that I appreciate. I hope he's not also an idiot, but that doesn't change the good he's done for me.

I've been thinking about cult stuff, and remembered that I was once involved in an organization that was accused of being a cult. The problem was that the leader was your typical megalomaniac, but almost none of the people working with this organization had ever met him or had much exposure even to media about him. They were involved because it was an amazing charitable organization doing amazing good work - that no one could doubt. It turned out he was a pedophile. A truly horrifyingly bad person. But the work of the organization had never been about him - AT ALL. Still, people outside the org who did know the founder decided that everyone working with it must be cult groupies, all brainwashed minions doing his bidding, simply because of association. The fact was, HE founded the group and started the amazing charitable work, even though he was a horrible person. The people were doing the work _because of the work_, not at all because of him, but they were still accused of being mindless minions.

The fact is, MOST people are sheep. Society wouldn't work well if that wasn't the case. And what I found then, and find now, is that the people crying "KOOLAID DRINKERS!" are just as sheep-y as the people allegedly drinking the koolaid, in both these particular cases. (Are there true cults? almost certainly. I'm distinguishing between those with true brainwashing (gradual elimination of the ability to think outside of what one is told) and those where people are just accused of it.) The people attacking Kruse have from the beginning engaged in childish, shrill behavior, in this case actually far more grouplike stupidity than those listening to him. They dogpile, they parrot, they don't engage in reasoned analysis, and that's why those listening to Kruse are unlikely to listen to them.

Well, there ARE legitimate criticisms of our "fearless leader," just as we all have legitimate criticisms of ourselves. How many of them are true, it's hard to know for sure. The question is, which are just character flaws, which discredit his ability to be a mentor to paleo patients, and which eliminate the credibility of any word out of his mouth at all?

Yes, he's Bongo. Yes, he has a big head and feeds off of people fawning over him - as mentioned by many of his critics, this is typical surgeon behavior. No, I SERIOUSLY doubt most of the rest of the accusations are true - stupid stuff like placing the Lance call, making up the surgery, trying to pass that picture off as himself, blah blah blah. If it is, we each need to know how that would change our looking to him for guidance or information, and be honest about it, not wistful. Overall, we do owe it to ourselves to keep our minds open, but that doesn't mean so far open our brains fall out, in either direction. Everyone read, think rationally and critically, decide what matters as far as how flawed a guru can be before you refuse to listen to him at all, look at your own personal results, check EVERYthing against other factual references (including history & anthropology), use caution, do what works for you, go with your gut, and good grief, maybe now that FTA is off the boat everyone will just ignore Kruse for a while and we can get on with healing our bodies with his good advice as we were already doing.

No doctor has ever changed my health this way. Ever.

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-05-19T13:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25456>

@MamaGrok: I completely agree. It can't be said more clearly. Every time one of us has scratch the scant research looking for something like a refutation to Dr. Kruse's ideas, we have found support for them.

Brilliant exposition.

### ruby — 2012-05-19T14:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25460>

wow, MamaGrok, that's more than I ever expected, thank you!

And I tend to agree with you.

I think the most important thing is, as you say, "Everyone read, think rationally and critically, decide what matters (...), look at your own personal results, check EVERYthing against other factual references (...), do what works for you, go with your gut..". And use commonsense, which as somebody already pointed out, is not that common, unfortunately.

So I read all the lamentations on various blogs about how Jack is going to damage the "movement", bring misery and suffering to people with his advice, which the biggest fault they could find with was that it was not his originally, but "stolen" (I mean did he really "steal" the idea of ice/snow baths from that Russian granny? is she upset and gonna sue? - video link on his FB). Come to think of it, did NASA invent the cold?

So after reading the lamentations and thinking about what I have gotten out of his blog and how could his advice possibly damage me (I don't care about the "movement" cos I will never be part of it, having a strong aversion to proselytizing and **organized** "religion" of any kind), I conclude that the overall balance is on the "+" side. I'd say most of my personal benefits came from his pointers rather than actual direct advice and a big chunk came from intelligent people's comments here. It may be because of my circumstances - I was never obese, only a bit overweight years ago, so I was never desperate enough to dive head on into any miraculous diet/lifestyle offered by a guru, no questions asked. I chanced upon his blog looking for health tweak ideas and I got them. From multiple sources in here, as I said above.

I don't agree all surgeons have inflated egos - I have several in my own close family and they are rather humble people and too busy publishing and researching stuff to run a blog and answer questions about numb toes (btw, it is NOT veiled criticism, Jack). However, as far as Paleo egos go, after the last few days of reading other people's stuff, I'm blown away by how far Jack lags behind in that department, though he is certainly way above average compared with the general population.

But that's ok, because I am not looking here for a guru or even somebody to develop my willpower with Adobe.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-19T16:22:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25473>

@mamag. Nice!

The funny thing to me with all the nit-picky and somewhat irrelevant criticism, is the underlying assumption that we care. GOOD FRIGGIN GOD, He's my age, I've been on the internets since THEY WERE INVENTED. Do I have some cyber\*\*\*\* out there I would die of embarrassment from? You betcha. I've done things I pray my child never ever finds out about. Do I evaluated myself today by that? Nope, and since my current self evaluations put me firmly in the "good" side - I recognize that other people can and do move on from actions and incidents in their life. If you are not failing, you are not moving forward!

By the mere fact that there is a identifiable "group" of people who think WE idolize JK and he's our GURU tells way more about them, than us. If he were a guru or god, then we very well may be devastated to find out he was bongo. As it is, I don't care because I don't elevate my relationship with Jack (or lack thereof) to something where he is letting me down by comments on football. Football is for idiots. So, I suppose that does lessen Jack in my preferences - but do I dismiss every idea because of that?

If this all turns to \*\*\*\*, I'll be let down - sure. But it won't be Jack that caused it or is responsible for it.

And since we've got him naked here to inspect (thank god it's not you and me under this judgemental eye right?) about the sanctionsfrom court. . Again, all context. If you've ever EVER been party to this kind of proceeding you know that being in a room (it's never tv like) and answering questions that get documented and turned and the records just never seem to reflect what I remember the reality was. And again, so irrelevant to the information I'm getting from him now.

Guess what, as a critical thinker and an EXPERT observer of human behavior - he's a bigger than life personality full of exaggeration to make his points. He's the more charasmatic and outgoing version of you. I guarantee he's not sitting there plotting some little mis-direction to make you freezerburn a patch of your fat tummy. I mean really.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-19T16:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25477>

Ruby, one of the silliest things I've seen is how the same people who say, "He's dangerous" say the next minute, "He says nothing new; he got it from everyone else."

Which is it, folks?

Clearly not all surgeons have god complexes. But they don't have that reputation for no reason. It's all too common, although certainly not universal. Again, the same people who said, "Surgeons are known for their megalomaniacal personalities" failed to notice that either that discredits most surgeons or none of them. It can't be used as an argument against only one of them, but that didn't stop folks from trying.

I agree with your sentiments about proselytizing and thinking this has to be some movement. I got into paleo because the science pointed me that way. I hope others find their way to the same facts, because facts is facts, and it will help them. If they ask me, I'll point them that way, but I'm not ever going to push it on anyone. Nor will I push my love or hatred for any one person on anyone. That's all disrespectful of our autonomy and dignity.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-19T17:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25499>

MamaG and Cavemam, nicely said and it echos I think what many \`followers\`are thinking I know it reflects many of the things I have thought about JK.

As many have said here, on the blog and elsewhere the biggest concern is that the kerfuffle about Jack\`s personality will overshadow and somehow minimize the message. As Marshall McLuhan said in 1964 **\`The medium is the message\`**

Truth be told Jack Kruse reminds me strongly of another Jack, Jackie Chan. Both have very large egos, need the constant feedback from fans, have to be the leader in their field and in the public eye, HATE being told what to do by others, even those with the best of intentions in mind, both have stated in public that they have few regrets in life and don\`t care what people say about them, then they say things that make their fans/followers scratch their heads and wonder \`did he really mean to say THAT? does he really want that posted on the net?\` (I heard this multiple times from JC\`s webmaster when he had an English blog, somehow I imagine the JK MM mutter the same thing ), they receive regular criticism from opponents who don\`t like the confident attitude and absolute certainty these men project, controversy follows these guys like a dog after a bone, both are Aries btw.

work is play to these men so a 70+ hour week is taking it easy and both have some strange obsession with long hair on women  and yet both are also incredibly philanthropic, willing to give time, money or expertise to support what they believe in.

JC\`s manager was asked to sum up his charge in a single sentence once and the response was simple: \`he is mostly a good man\`

I believe this to be true of Jack Kruse as well

but I will say, full well knowing this is likely to be dimissed, that JK need better handlers to help him refine his medium to a certain extent if he doesn\`t want the message marginalized by his detractors.

just my $0.02

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-25522>

Groan...I try and focus on the **GOOD**. Jack is very good at synthesizing disparate bits of data and seemingly conflicting theories into a coherent whole. He is laying the foundation for a practical theory the lay person can wrap their heads around. Like others (MamaGrok, Cavemam, etc.) I find that I am learning a tremendous amount about my body that is terrifically helpful to me. Like most others here I fact check, as best I am able, what he says with others I respect (Robb Wolf, Mark Sisson, Art DeVany, etc) and have found no major inconsistencies. All of this for only the price of a cookbook (because that is all I am into this with Jack - for now until the book comes out). Think about that one for a second...why in the hell would anyone buy a cookbook from a neurosurgeon...ha ha...???

Anyway, you have to take everyone as they are - the whole person! For me this has been a very positive relationship. Until he starts disseminating information that doesn't work for me, I'll continue to knowingly ignore the distractions and keep "drinking the cool aid" with lots of ice of course!!!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-23T01:18:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-26509>

Funny, I have found some good gurus, too. Flylady, Dave Ramsey, and Mark Sisson come to mind. Some middling ones, like the founder of RR, and one horrible one, although I wasn't following \*him\* or his personal advice at all.

Someone complained that Jack has his own image as his website logo. So does Dave Ramsey. Ever seen his books? What do you see at the top of every MDA page? Sisson. Flylady is on all her pages, nd is her own logo, lol, just a cartoon version because she was never comfortable with her actual image. Same thing is the reason KD isn't on the RR forums, too, I'm sure, since her dietary plan never got down the right ingredients for weight loss for most people.

Gurus usually aren't doing anything new, but have found a useful way to pull together and present info that others have not found. Sometimes one of those elements is much stronger than the other, and that weakens the guru's usefulness.

They're usually VERY confidant or arrogant (some exceptions), and the best ones give away most of their advice for free. All the ones I mentioned above do. They all also have some things to pay the bills. They all have plans that leave most people going, "Why the heck would you do THAT?!" yet they all have track records of legions of people whose lives have been dramatically improved by their work.

I think the main reason people are ticked off at Jack has almost nothing to do with his message or recommendations and almost everything to do with personality and style. It's the biggest difference I see. FlyLady is much more micromanaging and KD is much more controlling. KD censors her forums heavily, and many people have quit her program because of it, but there's no organized crusade against her, or people on the verge of forming an anti-cult support group.

There are a lot of valid criticisms about personality & style, but for me, none of them rise even remotely close to the level of "I don't want to hear his analysis of the science. He has nothing more to offer that could benefit me." I'm able to sort out the flaws from the facts, and even more, from the man. We're all flawed, VERY flawed, just some in more visible ways than others.

> **Jack Kruse;26646 wrote:** Who is KD?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-23T04:05:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-26560>

I have no clue who that is......at all. Never even heard of her.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-23T04:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-26564>

Yes, I'm just too lazy to type out her whole name and feel goofy calling PhD's Dr. I've talked about RR a lot on the monster thread because her principles for battling sugar addiction are (and bear in mind this program has been around at least a decade, and was rooted in treating alcoholics)

\- 20-30g protein each morning (depending on body weight), within an hour of rising, EVERY DAY.

\- Same amount of protein at each of the other meals

\- No snacking

\- Don't fear fat

\- Food journaling, certain vitamins (reasonable ones), and ... a potato before bed

\- At least one serving of whole grains per meal (AAAAND now we see why no one loses weight on RR, but many kick sugar, at least for a good long while)

Startling similarity, especially when you consider her rationale behind this is completely flawed, IMO. She says you have to load up on protein all day long so that when you eat the potato, it'll tie up all the AA's except tryptophan so that it can cross the blood-brain barrier to make serotonin. Because all sugar addicts are depressed and have been both sugar addicts and depressed since birth.

Whatever. I was never depressed a day in my life, and only became a sugar addict after using way too many antibiotics. BUT - the morning protein revolutionized my life (small wonder?), and the no-judgment detective-work food journaling helped me to tie food to physical and mental symptoms. Pretty cool. RR took me from bingeing weekly or so to maybe once every month or so. The 65g LR breakfast did the rest.

But her forums are censored like CRAZY. No talking by name about a sweet you bypassed today or you're talking drug talk (or whatever the lingo is), and no, we don't tell you these made up rules in advance; we just jump on you. She was very accessible, though, and certainly a step in the right direction for me.

For my first year or two at MDA, I totally lamented that no one was focusing on help for sugar addiction in the primal community. It was "Go primal and all will fall in line, or you just have a will power problem!" Nonsense.

Enter the LR, and the steps had enough substantive similarity with what had already worked for me, it was definitely worth a look. Jack has combined primal with the useful RR steps (without knowing about them, and from the correct scientific basis) to create what we cravers actually need for success.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-23T04:23:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1509/post-26566>

> **Jack Kruse;26652 wrote:** I have no clue who that is......at all. Never even heard of her.

I have the book, but it's been awhile since I read it. It points out the tranquillizing effects of eating potatoes, and compares them favorably to pharmaceuticals. No doubt there is more to it than that -- if I read through it again I'll get back here.

I think that the reason Doc has been catching so much flack is that (1) too many of his ideas actually WORK, in comparison to all these other "experts", and (2) his personal idiosyncrasies appear to offer a vulnerability for these frustrated people to exploit.

Think of it like this: their ideas are their stock in trade, and the source of their reputations --- but many don't work very well. Of course they don't want to admit that. Someone waltzes in with ten ideas for their one or two, and people try them and some (of course never all, but more than is usually the case) get results. Green envy, and denial, and the threat of loss of professional prestige. You might as well put itching powder up their drawers.

# Thread: Epicatechin...its what's in chocolate and lots of other things
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epicatechin-its-whats-in-chocolate-and-lots-of-other-things.1516/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-17T15:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epicatechin-its-whats-in-chocolate-and-lots-of-other-things.1516/post-24858>

Cocoa 'Vitamin' Health Benefits Could Outshine Penicillin [http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2007/03/070311202024.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2007/03/070311202024.htm)

ScienceDaily (Mar. 11, 2007) — The health benefits of epicatechin, a compound found in cocoa, are so striking that it may rival penicillin and anaesthesia in terms of importance to public health, reports Marina Murphy in Chemistry & Industry, the magazine of the SCI. Norman Hollenberg, professor of medicine at Harvard Medical School, told C&I that epicatechin is so important that it should be considered a vitamin.

Currently, there are only 13 essential vitamins. An increase in the number of vitamins would provide significant opportunity for nutritional companies to expand their range of products. **Flavanols like epicatechin are removed for commercial cocoas because they tend to have a bitter taste. So there is huge scope for nutritional companies to develop epicatechin supplements or capsules .

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/03/120302132259.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/03/120302132259.htm)

Cocoa May Enhance Skeletal Muscle Function

ScienceDaily (Mar. 2, 2012) — A small clinical trial led by researchers at UC San Diego School of Medicine and VA San Diego Healthcare System (VASDHS) found that patients with advanced heart failure and type 2 diabetes showed improved mitochondrial structure after three months of treatment with epicatechin-enriched cocoa. Epicatechin is a flavonoid found in dark chocolate.

**"The cristae had been severely damaged and decreased in quantity in these patients," said one of the senior investigators, Francisco J. Villarreal, MD, PhD of UC San Diego's Department of Medicine's Division of Cardiology. "After three months, we saw recovery -- cristae numbers back toward normal levels, and increases in several molecular indicators involved in new mitochondria production."**

[http://jp.physoc.org/content/early/2...09924.abstract](http://http://jp.physoc.org/content/early/2...09924.abstract)

(-)-EPICATECHIN ENHANCES FATIGUE RESISTANCE AND OXIDATIVE CAPACITY IN MOUSE MUSCLE

The flavanol (-)-epicatechin, a component of cacao (cocoa), has been shown to have multiple health benefits in humans. Using one year old male mice we examined the effects of 15 days of (-)-epicatechin treatment and regular exercise on: 1) exercise performance; 2) muscle fatigue; 3) capillarity; and 4) mitochondrial biogenesis in mouse hindlimb and heart muscles. Twenty-five male mice (C57BL/6N) were randomized into four groups: 1) water; 2) water-exercise (W Ex); 3) (-)- epicatechin ((-)-Epi); and 4) (-)-epicatechin-exercise ((-)-Epi Ex). Animals received 1 mg∙kg 1 of (-)-epicatechin or water (vehicle) via oral gavage (twice daily). Exercise groups underwent 15 days of treadmill exercise. Significant increases in treadmill performance (≈50%) and enhanced in situ muscle fatigue resistance (≈30%) were observed with (-)-epicatechin. Component of oxidative phosphorylation complexes, mitofilin, porin, and Tfam as well as mitochondrial volume and cristae abundance were significantly higher with (-)-epicatechin treatment for hindlimb and cardiac muscles than exercise alone. In addition, there were significant increases in skeletal muscle capillarity. The combination of (-)-epicatechin and exercise resulted in further increases in oxidative phosphorylation complexes proteins, mitofilin, porin, and capillarity than (-)-epicatechin alone. These findings indicate that (-)-epicatechin _alone_ or in combination with exercise induces an integrated response that includes structural and metabolic changes in skeletal and cardiac muscles resulting in greater endurance capacity. These results, therefore, warrant the further evaluation of the underlying mechanism of action of (-)-epicatechin and its potential clinical application as an exercise mimetic.**

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-17T15:23:13+0300

(-)-EPICATECHIN ENHANCES FATIGUE RESISTANCE AND OXIDATIVE CAPACITY IN MOUSE MUSCLE

The flavanol (-)-epicatechin, a component of cacao (cocoa), has been shown to have multiple health benefits in humans. Using one year old male mice we examined the effects of 15 days of (-)-epicatechin treatment and regular exercise on: 1) exercise performance; 2) muscle fatigue; 3) capillarity; and 4) mitochondrial biogenesis in mouse hindlimb and heart muscles. Twenty-five male mice (C57BL/6N) were randomized into four groups: 1) water; 2) water-exercise (W Ex); 3) (-)- epicatechin ((-)-Epi); and 4) (-)-epicatechin-exercise ((-)-Epi Ex). Animals received 1 mg∙kg 1 of (-)-epicatechin or water (vehicle) via oral gavage (twice daily). Exercise groups underwent 15 days of treadmill exercise. Significant increases in treadmill performance (≈50%) and enhanced in situ muscle fatigue resistance (≈30%) were observed with (-)-epicatechin. Component of oxidative phosphorylation complexes, mitofilin, porin, and Tfam as well as mitochondrial volume and cristae abundance were significantly higher with (-)-epicatechin treatment for hindlimb and cardiac muscles than exercise alone. In addition, there were significant increases in skeletal muscle capillarity. The combination of (-)-epicatechin and exercise resulted in further increases in oxidative phosphorylation complexes proteins, mitofilin, porin, and capillarity than (-)-epicatechin alone. **These findings indicate that (-)-epicatechin _alone_ _or in combination with exercise induces an integrated response that includes structural and metabolic changes in skeletal and cardiac muscles resulting in greater endurance capacity. These results, therefore, warrant the further evaluation of the underlying mechanism of action of (-)-epicatechin and its potential clinical application as an exercise mimetic._**

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-17T15:36:33+0300

### chocolate — 2012-05-17T15:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epicatechin-its-whats-in-chocolate-and-lots-of-other-things.1516/post-24868>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/05/120509123858.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/05/120509123858.htm)

Soybeans Soaked in Warm Water Naturally Release Key Cancer-Fighting Substance

Bean

Hari B. Krishnan and colleagues explain that the substance, Bowman-Birk Protease Inhibitor (BBI), has shown promise for preventing certain forms of cancer in clinical trials. Those human tests resulted from evidence of BBI's beneficial effects, including indications that BBI derived from the large amounts of soybeans in traditional Japanese diets might underpin low cancer mortality rates in Japan. However, the current method of extracting BBI from soybeans is time-consuming and involves harsh chemicals. The scientists set out to see if there might be a greener and more environmentally friendly way of obtaining BBI.

**They found that _soybean seeds incubated in water at 122 degrees Fahrenheit naturally release large amounts of BBI_ that can easily be harvested from the water. The protein appeared to be active, with tests showing that it stopped breast cancer cells from dividing in a laboratory dish. "The abundance of BBI in soybean seed exudates by incubating the seeds in warm water provides a simple and alternative method to isolate this low molecular weight protein," the researchers said.

The scientists acknowledge funding from the Agricultural Research Service of the U.S. Department of Agriculture.

I wonder if that's the magic temperature?

[http://swfrec.ifas.ufl.edu/docs/pdf/vegetable\_hort/trans\_ds2.pdf](http://http://swfrec.ifas.ufl.edu/docs/pdf/vegetable_hort/trans_ds2.pdf)

**Growers who are interested in a "chemical-free" alternative to fungicide treatment might want to try a hot-water soak. This

technique may be used on tomato seeds and cabbage seeds just before planting to destroy many diseases causing microorganisms

carried on and within seeds. Seeds should be soaked at 122 degrees F for 25 minutes. Use a large quantity of water with some type of

agitator to keep the water temperature as uniform as possible. Note: water temperatures and treatment times in excess of that

recommended may kill the embryo or reduce the percentage of seed germination. **Lower temperatures will not kill the disease

microorganisms. Immediately after this heat treatment, seed should be plunged into cold water to reduce the post treatment “cooking

effect”, then removed and planted promptly.**

Seed Treatments

Three-Mile (Sulphur) Hot Springs | Wells Nevada Hot Spring Trails ...

[www.trails.com/tcatalog\_trail.aspx?trailid=HGS271-289](http://http://www.trails.com/tcatalog_trail.aspx?trailid=HGS271-289)

Elevation Gain: Minimal. Trail Type: Unspecified. Water Temp: 122 degrees F** at the source, and 95 degrees F in the pool. Three-Mile (Sulphur) Hot Springs ...

San Juan Capistrano Hot Springs (Orange County, CA)

[www.ocf.berkeley.edu/~fricke/hotspring/sanjuan/](http://http://www.ocf.berkeley.edu/~fricke/hotspring/sanjuan/)

I don't remember the first time I heard of the San Juan Hotsprings, but I have ... area is pleasant (assuming you don't mind a weak odor of sulphur) and the water is .... an old soaking pool and the small hot springs pools where 122 degree water ...**

### chocolate — 2012-07-29T17:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epicatechin-its-whats-in-chocolate-and-lots-of-other-things.1516/post-41795>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/11/101110141247.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/11/101110141247.htm)

> Why Chocolate Protects Against Heart Disease
>
>
>
> ScienceDaily (Nov. 12, 2010) â€” Numerous studies have shown that cocoa has a protective effect against cardiovascular diseases. The reason for this has now been uncovered by researchers at LinkÃ¶ping University in Sweden. When a group of volunteers devoured a good-sized piece of dark chocolate, it inhibited an enzyme in their bodies that is known to raise blood pressure.
>
>
>
> The findings, now being published in Journal of Cardiovascular Pharmacology, were revealed by a group of drug researchers headed by Ingrid Persson. "We have previously shown that green tea inhibits the enzyme ACE, which is involved in the body's fluid balance and blood pressure regulation. Now we wanted to study the effect of cocoa, since the active substances catechins and procyanidines are related," says Ingrid Persson.
>
>
>
> The researchers recruited 16 healthy volunteer subjects for the study. They were not tobacco users and were not allowed to take any pharmaceuticals for two weeks. During the last two days they were not allowed to eat chocolate or anything containing similar compounds, including many kinds of berries and fruits, nor could they drink coffee, tea, or wine.
>
>
>
> When the study took place, everyone in the group -- ten men and six women between the ages of 20 and 45 -- ate 75 grams of unsweetened chocolate with a cocoa content of 72 percent. To analyze what happened with the ACE enzyme, blood samples were taken in advance and then a half hour, one hour, and three hours afterward.
>
>
>
> **In the sample taken three hours afterward, there was a significant inhibition of ACE activity. The average was 18 percent lower activity than before the dose of cocoa, fully comparable to the effect of drugs that inhibit ACE and are used as a first-choice treatment for high blood pressure.**
>
>
>
> When the activities of the enzyme decline, the blood pressure goes down with time. As expected, no such effect was found in the subjects. To show this, the study would have to continue over a longer period.
>
>
>
> Even though Ingrid Persson is a drug researcher, the object of her studies is not to design new pharmaceuticals.
>
>
>
> "Our findings indicate that changes in lifestyle with the help of foods that contain large concentrations of catechins and procyaninides prevent cardiovascular diseases," she says.

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2011/08/110829070555.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2011/08/110829070555.htm)

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/05/120531200822.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/05/120531200822.htm)

# Thread: Surgery + Antibiotics + Possible Infection?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-antibiotics-possible-infection.1519/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-17T19:15:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-antibiotics-possible-infection.1519/post-24942>

Hi Everyone!

I am scheduled for surgery in a few weeks to remove a wisdom tooth and a dentigerous cyst that developed out of the unerupted wisdom tooth. I know weird, huh? There's a 1% chance that someone would get this and I got it! I eat strict post-reset keto-paleo so no sugar or chance of sugar/grains in my house.I do CT 5-6 days a week for 45 min in 55-60 degree water. Have Hashimotos (take T3), a little hormonal imbalance (ED) and an hsCRP of around 2.0.

The doc has prescribed Keflex (Cephalexin) for any potential infection after the surgery....he wanted to script Penicillin but I'm allergic to it. I hate antibiotics and have been working on candida for over a month now and don't want to destroy and gains I have made.

My questions are:

1) Do I really need to take it?

2) If I take it what probiotics should I take (any particular strains or brands that are good)?

3) What about iodine? Has anyone used this as an antibiotic/antibacterial/antiviral med on a surgery site?

Thanks for your help!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-17T19:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-antibiotics-possible-infection.1519/post-24943>

Why not avoid the keflex and just use peridex mouthrinse. Likely a lot better for oral coverage and wont screw your gut up? Ask the surgeon.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-17T19:43:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-antibiotics-possible-infection.1519/post-24947>

Thank you Dr. Kruse! I'll ask the surgeon what he thinks.

I have been CTing the jaw area twice a day and will continue after surgery too. I'm hoping to NOT have to use the Percocet at all because of the ice.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-jacks-not-a-big-liar-afterall.1523/post-25007>

"The researchers found greater amounts of leptin in hibernating than in active bears. The first paragraph, in the first osteoporosis blog, is exactly what this team just spent millions of taxpayer dollars (probably) to prove:

"RULE 1. If one is leptin resistant, Wolffâ€™s law is null and void, and you are at

very high risk for a fractured vertebrae or hip/wrist. This means that normal

osteoporotic treatments and exercise will not heal or strengthen a bone until

the underlyig pathophysiology is repaired first. When a person has high levels

of leptin, it eventually drives cortisol higher and this stimulates even more

inflammatory cytokines from cells. As this occurs, LR develops all over the

body. Cortisol is one of the major hormones involved in the sympathetic nervous

system. When cortisol is chronically high, as I told you in the Hormone 101

blog, itâ€™s bad news. When someone is leptin resistant, they block osteocalcinâ€™s

main function and this causes osteoporosis. This is one major reason why fat

people lose their bone. It also definitely proves that Wolffâ€™s law is null and

void when you are LR. Even resistance exercise maybe harmful when this occurs.

Bone only strengthens when the underlying hormonal terroir is working properly.

In LR, it is seriously broken."

# Thread: Ever heard of people sensitive to coconut oil?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-18T03:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25044>

I have to ask............if anyone's ever posted on here that they don't do well with coconut oil. I have tried incorporating it into my diet before and always think I gain weight on it. I have never liked coconut. It's one of the very few foods I just don't care for. Maybe it's all in my head, but wanted to ask the question. Is it possible I don't like it because my body doesn't like it? I know, maybe I'm getting "out there". Just putting it out there for your thoughts!

### chocolate — 2012-05-18T03:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25058>

I screwed up somehow. I guess I didn't get the memo.....I was using coconut milk (not fat) and I wasn't gaining weight. I was gaining inflammation.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-18T03:41:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25064>

Interesting! Is that supposed to be inflammatory?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-18T03:51:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25071>

some people cant tolerate CO. People who use HCG have lots of trouble with this fat.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-18T03:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25076>

Good to know! In that case, what would you recommend instead, Dr. Kruse?

### Guest — 2012-05-18T04:08:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25080>

I didn't like coconut oil at first but love what it is doing for me now. One must adhere to the whole protocol before they can really be qualified to judge a certain food. But then if I were on a narcotic drug I might think anything is possible.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-18T05:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25095>

I get migraines with CO. I have tried it repeatedly and 60-80% of the time, a headache comes on within a few hours. Sigh. Good thing butter agrees with me!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-18T21:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25276>

Yes, I guess I'll go to butter right now.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-18T21:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-heard-of-people-sensitive-to-coconut-oil.1526/post-25291>

Butter is great, ghee even better. This is true as long as you don't have any allergies/intolerances to diary. Also no butter if you have autoimmune diseases like Hashimotos (as many on this forum have), Celiacs, etc.

# Thread: Quercetin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quercetin.1527/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-18T03:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quercetin.1527/post-25048>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22438067](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22438067)

Quercetin increased bioavailability and decreased methylation of green tea polyphenols in vitro and in vivo.

The extensive methylation of green tea polyphenols (GTPs) in vivo may limit their chemopreventive potential. We investigated whether quercetin, a natural inhibitor of catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) and multidrug resistance proteins (MRPs), will differentially increase the intracellular concentration and decrease the methylation of GTPs in different cancer cell lines. Intrinsic COMT activity was lowest in lung cancer A549 cells, intermediate in kidney 786-O cells and highest in liver HepG2 cells. Quercetin increased the cellular absorption of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) four-fold in A549 cells with a decreased methylation rate from 63 to 19%, 2-fold in 786-O cells with a decreased methylation from 97% to 56%, while no significant effect was observed in HepG2 cells. The combination significantly decreased the activity and protein expression of COMT and decreased the protein expression of MRP1 compared to individual treatments. The combination exhibited the strongest increase in antiproliferation in A549 cells, an intermediate effect in 786-O cells and lowest effect in HepG2 cells. The effect of quercetin on bioavailability and metabolism of GTPs was confirmed in vivo. Severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) mice were administered brewed green tea (GT) and a diet supplemented with 0.4% quercetin alone or in combination for 2 weeks. We observed a 2- to 3-fold increase of total and non-methylated EGCG in lung and kidney and an increasing trend in liver. In summary, combining quercetin with GT provides a promising approach to enhance the chemoprevention of GT. Responses of different cancers to the combination may vary by tissue depending on the intrinsic COMT and MRP activity.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-18T03:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quercetin.1527/post-25051>

It also is helpful in reducing itching from histamine and works in unison with resveratrol syngergistically

### chocolate — 2012-11-25T18:45:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quercetin.1527/post-65592>

http://www.hindawi.com/journals/jbb/2009/419891/

The Phytoestrogen Quercetin Impairs Steroidogenesis and Angiogenesis in Swine Granulosa Cells In Vitro

Â 

AbstractExperimental evidence documents that nutritional phytoestrogens may interact with reproductive functions but the exact mechanism of action is still controversial. Since quercetin is one of the main flavonoids in livestock nutrition, we evaluated its possible effects on cultured swine granulosa cell proliferation, steroidogenesis, and redox status. Moreover, since angiogenesis is essential for follicle development, the effect of the flavonoid on VascularÂ  Endothelial Growth FactorÂ  output by granulosa cells was also taken into account. **Our data evidence that quercetin does not affect granulosa cell growth while it inhibits progesterone production and modifies estradiol
Â 
Â Â  Â Â Â  Â 
Â Â  Â Â Â  Â Â Â  Â 

Â Â  Â Â Â  Â 
Â Â  Â 
Â **production in a dose-related manner.** Additionally, the flavonoid interferes with the angiogenic process by inhibiting VEGF production as well as by altering redox status. Since steroidogenesis and angiogenesis are strictly involved in follicular development, these findings appear particularly relevant, pointing out a possible negative influence of quercetin on ovarian physiology.** Therefore, the possible reproductive impact ofÂ  the flavonoidÂ  should be carefully considered in animal nutrition.

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-05-20T14:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thread-for-the-clueless.1536/post-25666>

"an explanation for the way things are" it is. Believe me when I say Factor X is irrelevant for present life as it is now (not that it wouldn't be different if it hasn't been, and clearly it has been a special key in the puzzle for Dr. Kruse) and somewhat _disappointing (I am not the only one to even have talked about it without pointing it out with two letters)... not so its _consequences (I am not completely convinced about the adjective_ here, though) nowadays:

-   Much higher quantity of junk DNA on eutherians than in birds (I only have a comment in [this entry](http://http://scienceblogs.com/pharyngula/2010/05/junk_dna_is_still_junk.php)): yes, birds are quite more perfect

_

-   , with their superior mitochondria, but it seems that we eutherians have won being more adaptable.
-   Retrotransposons and its great abundance in primates: see for example [this paper](http://http://rcordaux.voila.net/pdfs/42.pdf).
    Junk DNA coming from viruses: does primates with their [GULO mutation](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L-gulonolactone_oxidase) are more susceptible to increase their junk DNA because of it? I know, I know... it would close the circle for me (and I am completely convinced of the [antiviral potency of vitamin C in high doses](http://http://perfecthealthdiet.com/2010/09/fighting-viral-infections-by-vitamin-c-at-bowel-tolerance/)). It is a quite testable hypothesis though: if frugivorous bats and guinea pigs had a higher amount of junk DNA than the rest of eutherians (like primates do), it would point toward it.

Just as Dr. Kruse says, we will have a lot to talk about the implications of the epigenetic speed up.
