>
> I want to know if I'm ovulating since AN is supposed to destroy GnRH which results in amenorrhea (no menses) and prevent the LH surge that's needed for ovulation to happen. I didn't start menstruating til I was 27... cycle has been pretty regular for the last few years (so GnRH has fixed itself) but I'm still unsure if the LH surge part is happening correctly. How do I find out? I do get the mucus changes, well, at least sometimes...

you can ovulate w/o menses.... \*waves hand\* go preggers w/o menses  trying both times too! egg white cm (stringy/cleary, runny like an egg white) occurs around ovulation... best way to verify -is to check cervix height/opening and am basal body temp before getting out of bed...

### fitness@home — 2012-06-05T03:27:00+0300

I have read this entire thread with interest, as I am on compounded BHRT (and have been for about 8 years). It has always been in pill form and I am ready to try another form of delivery. Have an appt with a new Gyno that is also a Naturopath on Friday.

Besides compounded cremes, this doctor also offers BHRT pellets. Does ANYONE have experience with them? or maybe a friend or family member? I am interested in any feedback - good, bad, or indifferent. Once the pellets are implanted you are pretty much stuck with them for 3-6 months, until the hormones have been utilized by your body.

Thanks in advance,

Dawn

### herringsalad — 2012-06-06T15:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-29698>

> **colleencoble;28746 wrote:** Get Uzzi Reiss's book Natural Hormone Balance for Women. She needs progesterone not Mirena. I had this myself. I lost my first ovary at 14 (ruptured) and had a cyst the size of an orange removed from my other ovary at 22. I was told the ovary wouldn't last long and if I wanted another child, to do so asap. I had my daughter and 6 mos later had a hysterectomy as the entire ovary became covered in cysts. I've made hormone study a huge priority in my life. If I knew then what I know now, I wouldn't have lost that ovary. I needed my hormones balanced including thyroid even back then. So she needs a good doctor who understands hormones asap.

Colleen,

I just wanted to thank you for this recommendation. I got Reiss' book overnight from Amazon Prime and devoured the entire thing in about three hours. I then spent about seven hours shaking with anger. So cross about doctors who have consistently failed both my sister and I. When I had fibroids removed, no mention of estrogen excess was made. So mad that my sister's surgeon never even mentioned natural progesterone or suggested testing her hormone levels. So furious that if wasn't for my dogged determination and obssessive pouring over this website, I'd never even know about bio-identical hormones. So angry that none of the British doctors/gynies/surgeons I and my sister have seen, have any knowledge of BHRT when it was a British woman, Katrina Dalton, who started the research in the 1950s. To quote the great movie: "I'm mad as hell and I'm not going to take it anymore!"

But no point in simply being cross: action is needed. So I've found a bio-identical hormone doctor here in London - Dame Shirley Bond - and I've booked an appointment. I'd like to make sure that I've got my own estrogen dominance under control and see what options I've got. I'm also going pre-vet her for my sister. I'm impressed with her website so fingers crossed.

And the book made me realise just how much I've achieved by following MDA and most of Dr K's recommendations over the last six months. My periods used to ruin my life for a week out of every month. Now I only have pain for 40 minutes rather than being woken up at 4am every morning for a week. I'm no longer bloated. And I don't have those same feelings of heaviness or faitgue. I've also lost 12 pounds and I'm wearing clothes that I haven't woren for six years.

So thanks to Colleen, Dr K and everyone else here that does their dirty best to let us women know that we can be in control of our hormones not our hormones in control of us.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-06T16:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-29706>

So glad you have a plan of action, herring! And that you're feeling better already!

### colleencoble — 2012-06-07T16:14:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-29891>

Gretch, is your husband there or any other person? If so, have him take the fleshy part of your hand between your thumb and forefinger in a pincher move. There will be a sore spot in there and a little swelling. Have him find it and squeeze very very hard for about 5 minutes. You'll know he's found the right spot when the migraine starts to ease. He may have to try the other hand because it's generally based in one hand or the other. When the headache eases, he can stop and you'll find the last bits of it should ebb away in the next half hour or so.

### Lyndra — 2012-06-10T03:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-30362>

I think the cost is going to depend heavily on dosage and the form of BHRT. I started on bilingual estrogen and progesterone capsules and gradually upped my dosage over a few months while we were sorting out the effects. I know the tablets & capsules are more expensive than cream and certainly taking 2-3 per day of each is costing me way more than having them compound one formulation. I just had a recheck with the doctor I got a 2nd opinion from and she said I could probably get estrogen/progesterone cream for $50 for 2 months. That would be a huge bargain over what I am paying now.

### Destiny — 2012-06-13T23:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-30976>

> **DarleenMB;28514 wrote:** Colleen, do you know how much you're taking? When you say you're cycling does that mean you do two weeks of progesterone, increasing the dosage to peak and then tapering back off? This is what my cycle looks like. The numbers equate to lines on the little plastic syringe so "4" would be "4 lines." the estradiol, 1 line - 1 mg and for the progest 1 line = 20mg. And those doses are done 2x a day. 12 hours apart.
>
>
>
> Day - estradiol - progesterone
>
> 1 - 4 - 0
>
> 2 - 4 - 0
>
> 3 - 4 - 0
>
> 4 - 4 - 0
>
> 5 - 4 - 0
>
> 6 - 6 - 0
>
> 7 - 6 - 0
>
> 8 - 6 - 0
>
> 9 - 7 - 0
>
> 10 - 7 - 0
>
> 11 - 7 - 0
>
> 12 - 8 - 0
>
> 13 - 2 - 0
>
> 14 - 2 - 4
>
> 15 - 3 - 4
>
> 16 - 3- 8
>
> 17 - 3 - 8
>
> 18 - 4 - 10
>
> 19 - 4 - 10
>
> 20 - 4 - 12
>
> 21 -4 - 14
>
> 22 - 4- 12
>
> 23 - 4 - 10
>
> 24 - 4 - 10
>
> 25 - 4 - 8
>
> 26 - 4 - 8
>
> 27 - 4 - 4
>
> 28 - 4 - 4
>
>
>
> I don't know if that will help you at all but I figured as an FYI it couldn't hurt.

Darlene,

This is a very useful information. Would you know the strength of your estradiol and progesterone?

I am on the Triest cream that is 0.25mg/ml. That mean that I get only 0.5 mg/ml of estradiol (15%), estrone (15%) and estriol (70%) a day. I feel better but not great. When I talked to my BHRT doctor, her response was that she wants me to take as little as possible to keep me safe.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-13T23:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-30982>

Triest is perfectly safe as long as you are balancing it with progesterone. You want balanced and not stopped from optimal because of a doctor's fears.

Megs, hope they get that straightened out soon! I'm feeling awesome now that I'm back to my usual and NOT cycling. No more headaches at all and good energy again.

### Pamela — 2012-06-18T23:38:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-32011>

As I mentioned a few weeks ago I signed up with Dzlogic (Dzugan) and it took several weeks but now I have all my supplements and my hormone creams. I've been taking everything and have noticed some nice changes, mainly in mood. My main reason for going on the program was due to sleep problems that I knew were from body changes since I never used to have sleep problems. But since starting all the supplements and cream and knowing they are in the exact right amount for me I'm feeling calmer and generally happier and don't go off as easy. My sleep has improved also though still not sleeping all the way through the night. I'm only about a week in to the program so expect it to get better.

It is an expense but I felt it was worth it and I wouldn't keep paying other doctors and tons of other supplements to not get the results I wanted.

HI, Do you take the hormones 2 x a day? I am thinking of trying progesterone like this instead of waiting for day 15. last set of labs we ran on day 21 of my cycle... my P:E ratio is moving up, but no where near optimal yet. as far as inferring you're low all month, the only way you could know that is if you tested btwn CD3-5 and around CD 21 if your cycle is normally 28-30days in length, to get a real picture from what I understand....

That is interesting that P is only at night as I thought it needs to be used every 12 hours. How many milligrams of progesterone is in the cream when you take .2ml? Would that be 20mg per .2ml? ### Shijin13 — 2012-06-26T23:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-33769>

> **freshveggies;33905 wrote:** That is interesting that P is only at night as I thought it needs to be used every 12 hours. How many milligrams of progesterone is in the cream when you take .2ml? Would that be 20mg per .2ml? I can figure it out if you tell me how many mg/ml of progesterone is on the script. I think I am goinig to visit with the pharmacist here as he is helping other women since the docs just don't know. Does Dzugan put most women on P all month long? Thank you.

the P I take is 5% formula 1 gel 50mg/ml.

I take .2ml during totm (so 50mg/ml x.2ml =10mg)

.4ml for 10days after totm (50mg/ml x.4ml = 20mg)

.8ml for the remainder of my cycle (50mg/ml x.8ml = 40mg)

Dzugan works w/each individual - I Know Colleen is on at least .8ml all the time. others it varies based upon labs, and how they feel.

I've been thinking I actually need to look at starting out at .2ml for menses, .4ml for 5days, then .6ml for 5days, .8 for 5 days, .10ml for 5days, then back down to .8for the remainder of my cycle... we'll see what Dzugan says.... I really need to bring my P up to get my P:E ratio balanced properly...

> I just think I am looking more estrogen dominant. Turning it to mush like my mom. I don't want that for me. My 2 doctors actually thougth a 3.6 number for progesterone was ok. I just kind of wanted to tear into them. I know my body wants and need more progesterone than that.

### Destiny — 2012-07-20T14:17:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-39634>

Just found this article that came from The Green Willow Tree and it concerns me:

One of the rationales for using large amounts of progesterone is the belief that progesterone will convert into other hormones in the body, as needed. For example, progesterone is supposed to convert to estrogen and testosterone. And in fact, this is how the progesterone produced by our ovaries does work.However, with the advent of the more accurate saliva hormone assays, we have learned that there is at least one important distinction between the way that ovarian progesterone and Progesterone USP work in the body. While ovarian progesterone does have the ability to "cascade", or convert into other hormones in the body, there is no evidence that Progesterone USP cascades in the body, beyond a possible slight conversion to testosterone. Upjohn, one of the manufacturers of Progesterone USP, has admitted that this is true, perhaps due to the laboratory processing. Therefore, if a woman takes in more than she needs, she can develop excessive amounts of progesterone.

What are the effects of excess progesterone? Symptoms can begin in a very subtle manner, and can mimic many other types of conditions. The first sign could be depression and/or lethargy. Women who are still menstruating may have anovulatory cycles, perhaps because too much progesterone can actually block estrogen. Memory loss, fluid retention, and protracted menstrual hemorrhaging have been observed in some cases. Joy Bucknell from Dr. Lynn August's office in New Fane, Vermont, has written about the problem of progesterone excess. To quote from her article:

"Initially, most women feel a calming effect when they use progesterone. However, after approximately eight months of high active progesterone levels a clinical depression may develop. Often times the cause of this depression is not attributed to the use of the progesterone cream. The second downside of high active progesterone is its effect on active cortisol levels in the body . . . **High levels of active progesterone . . . cause a significant increase in free active cortisol . . . High active cortisol over the long term can result in hunger and sugar or carbohydrate cravings, weight gain around the waist, reduced muscle mass, bone thinning, food sensitivities and allergies, reduced athletic endurance, yeast overgrowth, reduced thyroid function, insomnia, PMS, and if not corrected, eventual exhaustion and chronic fatigue."**

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-27T01:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-41393>

Hi BHRT experts.... next week I am having a long-awaited appointment with the only doctor I can find on the south island of New Zealand who seems to know anything about BHRT. I have a history of breast cancer (2 years ago), am 46 and perimenopausal (still cycling, though not charting so not sure how often I'm ovulating, cycles still regular). I did a blood test (self pay) to get E2 and P ratios, though I did it on the wrong day, so results are skewed toward higher E. However, my P levels were too low to detect any in my blood! And my E was WAY over the range. Not a surprise, but still...

I would like to ask ANY and ALL advice, questions and input from you ladies who have experience with BHRT... if you were me, what would you be asking your doc? (I have read Suzanne Somers and Uzzi Reiss and had a consult with Dr K.) I want to get full E testing (1, 2 and 3) as well as E2 on the right day of my cycle tested, and also the 2/16 hydroxy ratio and I want to trend these.

What would you expect to hear from a competent doc? What would you think she should be talking about? BHRT of both E (estriol and estradiol) and P (in massive quantities)? Cycling amounts? Creams only? DHEA supplement too? (I tested DHEA and it is in the lower 1/3 of the range... started 7 Keto (you can't get DHEA here without a prescription) about 20 days ago.)

Dr K told me I am a 'doctor's worst nightmare' because of my age and my history of breast cancer. I feel like I really need to be prepared because the stakes are high and no one seems to know what they are talking about.

I would so greatly appreciate any thoughts of any kind (including any warning bells I should look out for) you have.

Thanks!

Lauren

>
> What would you expect to hear from a competent doc? What would you think she should be talking about? BHRT of both E (estriol and estradiol) and P (in massive quantities)? Cycling amounts? Creams only? DHEA supplement too? (I tested DHEA and it is in the lower 1/3 of the range... started 7 Keto (you can't get DHEA here without a prescription) about 20 days ago.)
>
>
>
> Dr K told me I am a 'doctor's worst nightmare' because of my age and my history of breast cancer. I feel like I really need to be prepared because the stakes are high and no one seems to know what they are talking about.
>
>
>
> I would so greatly appreciate any thoughts of any kind (including any warning bells I should look out for) you have.
>
>
>
> Thanks!
>
> Lauren

I think you've got to ensure they look at the whole picture. You are Also coming to BHRT to help you get balanced. That might be bi-est or Tri-est, though w/your history they might just go w/Pg (either in cream form or a combo of cream & prometrium).

Ask the BHRT doc if they've got any former cancer px using BHRT that you could speak w/. Ask about how they monitor supplementation w/DHEA for those who've had cancer. Do they use it? What about 7-Keto? How often do they test?

I'm sure I'll think of more but it's tim to turn off the mismatch for the night. Good luck.

I just stopped my BHRT (only for a week as I should) after a few months of continuous use. This is the first time, I feel no ill side effects from stopping. The only thing is that my FBG started climbing into a mid 100's again only after 3 days of stopping the hormones (estrogen and progesterone).

I started BHRT in February. That was the only month when my FBG went down into 80's. I guess my body was shocked by having hormones available? Then after, it started to climb into 90's again and it has been between 90-102 ever since. More in 90's than in 100's.

Anybody is experiencing this?

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-27T19:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-41541>

no, but the circadian cycle can impact if you are a E-collector/E-dominate I think it depends where you're at in your phase as a woman. If you're a normal cycling woman w/o issues - you're shouldn't be E-Dominate (However in today's modern world every woman probably has some level of E-dominance). If you have PCOS, or are transitioning to peri-menopause, or menopause - you're not making enough Pg, but you're still making E. When you have PCOS, peri-menopause, or menopause - you don't sleep well, when you don't sleep well, you can be come LR, - which means you're not releasing melatonin at the right time, or making prolactin for autophage (B/c modern lights interfere w/the release of both melatonin & prolactin) -when you don't hit autophage, you don't make Human growth hormone. when you don't hit autophage you stress your adrenals b/c your circadian cycle is off! when your adrenals are off you're LDL is then converted to cortisol as part of there response... and your T4 is turned to RT3 instead of T3. This is why Jack has harped about fixing the adrenals first... but for PCOS, perimenopausal and menopausal women - its harder b/c you have to fix everything together...

Cycloset -helps fix the circadian cycle & Insulin Resistance. BHRT supports the Hormonal cascade

I do have to get this tested again to see where my cholesterol is at now since I have some FT3. I would think this means some things are changing.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-02T00:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-55697>

This import from a [previous blog post](http://http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/) might help you: The principal hormone replacement therapies that can increase the secretion of cortisol and/or its action are by increasing the anabolic steroid and sex steroids like testosterone, dihydrotestosterone, and estrogen. This is true in both men and women. These are both potent stimulators of reestablishing the normal diurnal cortisol production in humans. The reason is quite simple. When the more distal sex hormones are all in adequate supply, very little shunting of the substrate hormone, pregnenolone, is taken away from adrenal cortisol production pathway. The same is true of DHEA levels to a lesser degree. This is classically seen in a pregnenolone steal syndrome. Most people with adrenal fatigue suffer some sort of co morbid hormone issue.

Many people ask me about the importance of thyroid hormone in this clinical scenario. Thyroid hormones are only mild stimulators of cortisol production pathways in humans. Much stronger biochemical inhibiters of cortisol production are high growth hormone levels (great body comp), hyperthyroidism (low TSH levels), and those with melatonin excess (exogenous supplementation). So people with high IGF-1 levels, low TSH levels, and those who sleep well, generally have low cortisol levels. Mild inhibitors to cortisol production are those women on oral estrogen. BCP’s make adrenal fatigue more likely, as does fludrocortisone, and those with elevated aldosterone levels. People with high aldosterone levels have higher blood pressures. They also have lowered thyroid hormones, lowered sex steroid hormones as well. People with low blood pressures have lower cortisol levels and lower aldosterone levels. When people have low cortisol production in the morning, and increasing cortisol levels at night, there are many ways to naturally boost the cortisol levels to balance the circadian cycle back to normal.

### maggie — 2012-10-02T03:19:47+0300

I just got some labs back in prep for an educational consult with Doctor Kruse. The DHEA-S and Free Testosterone numbers are crazy! I had called LEF before the test to ask about taking hormones the day of the blood draw and they told me OK at least two hours before the blood draw, and that's what I did.

I'm with Dzugan (DzLogic), and I'm taking both DHEA and 7-keto, but NOT testosterone.

DHEA-S > 1000 \[!\] range 9.4-246.0 This one was flagged **abnormal** Ya think??

Free Testosterone (Direct) 3.8 range 0.0-1.8

In my previous test in April, DHEA-S was within range, but testosterone was also high at 106(ref 3-41) and I've been taking saw palmetto. Both the testosterone readings are a little over twice the top of the reference range.

Has anyone else experienced readings like this, really far out of range? Should I just get tested again right away? I'm a bit freaked out. I wrote an update to Dzugan right away and hopefully will hear back soon.

### maggie — 2012-10-03T03:56:50+0300

Another question for you experienced bHRT folks. Does anyone know how to figure the quartile for PG and E2? I'm post menopausal and don't know if I should use the post meno range that Labcorp gives or whether I'm supposed to be aiming for the values of a younger and still cycling woman. ### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-11T06:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-57530>

> **kathylu;58837 wrote:** That's one of my questions too...my most recent E2g ratio was 145. Better than it was previously, but not the 400-500 figure I have seen talked about here. However, my absolute levels of e2 and pg are still quite low, definitely menopausal. So, let's say my pg level increases and my ratio starts to look better...but I am still at menopausal absolute hormone levels. Then I would need to work on raising my estrogen level as well, right? Even though that's a bit scary...you know, me, the estrogen collector, waiting for the epithelial cancer to strike (even though my total estrogen level is exceedingly low).

I know we all have specific contexts so take this with a grain of salt... my reading (and a certain forward-thinking-optimal doctor) suggested to me that being on both P and E could be a good idea. You can be on just estriol, or estriol with estradiol (or all three, including estrone). I think the issue isn't higher estrogen alone, but how it's broken down (so this is where the 2/6/16 hydroxy knowledge is important). This is, of course, within the context of an optimised Pg:E2 ratio.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-11T21:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-57646>

> **Maggie;58901 wrote:** What does 2/1/16 hydroxy refer to, please?
>
>
>
> I recently read in Dzugan's book on migraines that triest is 80-90% estriol--at least the compound he uses for migraines.

If you look at how estrogens are metabolised, they can break down into harmful or non-harmful versions (that's my very non-scientific explanation). Estriol is the weakest form of estrogen, but also the safest (it doesn't degrade into potentially harmful sorts). But if you are an E collector or have concerns about breast cancer risks, you can be tested to see how much 2 hydroxy, 4 hydroxy and 16 hydroxy you have (what percentages you're converting into). I'll try to find the link to the article that explains this. Sorry I'm not terribly articulate on this.

I started getting a few spots of acne on my chin and neck a couple of days before ovulation. It has now started to subside on day 22 of my cycle. (I thought it was perhaps due to my T, which is at the upper end of the range, but I stopped all 7 Keto weeks ago and haven't added in anything else... though new doc just prescribed 25mg DHEA but I haven't started yet. What say ye?

### stacyj — 2012-10-19T15:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59150>

Anyone else been "diagnosed" as having a thickened endometrium? I had a transvag ultrasound for heavy, irregular periods, and was told this was the result- and immediately an endometrium biopsy was done. I went to this gyno because he supposedly specializes in BHRT. I realize you have to jump through all of the hoops/tests, but he really keeps pushing an ablation on me. First visit- "you are on the young side for becoming menopausal" (I'm 43), an ablation will stop the heavy bleeding. Second visit- your labs indicate becoming menopausal- here have a biopsy, then we'll talk D&C or ablation. Ack!

Anyone have experience with this? I get ruling out cancer, but my understanding is a thick endometrium can be caused by being estrogen dominant- I am perimenopausal, and the labs show E dominance......so not happy with the push for a surgical "solution". sigh.....I really do not care for most dr's.

By evening I can feel my hormones wear off and I don't feel I can make it through the night. Why does Dzugan feel that we should go all night without any and how can some women take the same during the day and night and still sleep good.

I don't sleep well and I am trying to figure out if the hormones are involved. I am peri-menopausal and passed the time my mom went into menopause. Dr. Uzzie says that the first thing to look at for insomnia is low Estradiol. That I have, but I just wonder if I don't have the right ratios to help me sleep. I go to sleep fine, but then have been waking at 2:20 and can't go back to sleep.

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-21T03:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59420>

> **KiwiLauren;60950 wrote:** If you think it's an absorption issue, you can also ask for a different route... sublingual or a vaginal troche. May be worth a try.

FYI: This website says women should NOT use vaginal application b/c it can expose their partner to too much estrogen, or probably any kind of hormone including progesterone. Scroll down to bottom of estriol cream details.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-21T16:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59456>

I was working with a doctor that would only use vaginal form. Capsule with progesterone mixed with olive oil. She said just put it in after the fact. I have read that the creams are fully absorbed within an hour and you can safely get wet after that.

TO KandC88-- My estrogen is 1% per gram of 90% estriol/7% estradiol/3% esterone. This is one of Dzugans. I also have 1.25%

I put on .1ml per TOM .3-.4ml next 10 days and .2-.25 ml until the end of the cycle. I use between 10-50mg of progesterone a day. He has me taking hormones only in the am. I have been able to take them that way the last 2 days. I have always put them on every 12 hours, but I am trying to do what my plan says to see how I feel on that. There is a reason for his theory, I just don't know all the details and that is what I would like to know from them. Hope this helps. He has a book about Migraines that can give you some direction as to how he doses hormones. That helped me a lot when I was doing it on my own, until I went with his program. It is so individual too.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-21T17:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59462>

I'm thinking after my last sets of labs... I don't absorb the Pg creams well via the skin... I think I need either oral (sublingual) or possible vaginal combined w/transdermal pg.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-21T17:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59465>

> **Shijin13;61011 wrote:** I'm thinking after my last sets of labs... I don't absorb the Pg creams well via the skin... I think I need either oral (sublingual) or possible vaginal combined w/transdermal pg.

What was you lab for P. I was following your thread, but forgot what that was. What amount were you taking and what was your lab previously without taking P?

From my experience, I did not like the subligual liquid because I could feel it go in and out of my system fast. Did not feel normal. The vaginal does work got my levels up, but I did not feel as natural on it. If you do try those routes, you will want to talk with the compounding pharmacist to make sure they don't add anything extra to it. Oral P is really bitter and they have to add an anti-bitter substance to it and stevia. I had mine with 50 % less than others would need. Maybe the troche would work better. Dissolves slower. I have a friend on that and she loves them. My pharmacists didn't know you could use olive oil as the carrier for the vaginal P until I took one to him and showed him how it was done. You put it in a gelatin capsule and it works. He had been using polyethene glycol. Bad stuff.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-21T20:05:57+0300

If you do try those routes, you will want to talk with the compounding pharmacist to make sure they don't add anything extra to it. Oral P is really bitter and they have to add an anti-bitter substance to it and stevia. I had mine with 50 % less than others would need. Maybe the troche would work better. Dissolves slower. I have a friend on that and she loves them. My pharmacists didn't know you could use olive oil as the carrier for the vaginal P until I took one to him and showed him how it was done. You put it in a gelatin capsule and it works. He had been using polyethene glycol. Bad stuff.

I've been on Pg for close to 9mths. prior to starting BHRT my pg was .5ng/mL.... 4mths into taking BHRT it was up to 19ng/mL... close to 8 mths in it is 10.9ng/mL....

I upped my pg 2mths ago b/c of wicked migraines- so I'm now taking 5% Pg .6ml for TOTM, 1.0ml for 10 days (+.1ml Tri-Est for 10days), then 1.4ml for the remainder of my cycle.

I'm pretty sure that Dzugan will instruct the compounding Rx that he uses w/the proper formularly depending on what we're going to do. I really think the PCOS is interfering w/the absorbing the Pg gel

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-21T20:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59499>

The dosing is going to depend on the mg/dl of the the Tri-est. My triest (90/7/3) is 1.25mg/ml gel. I take .1ml you'll have to run the math to get the actually amt based upon what the dose is. not everyone is on the 1.25mg/ml gel...some are higher some are lower. it's dependent upon your labs and your specific cirumstances

Looks like my current dosing is pretty strong! I'm finding all of the info very useful. Keep it coming ladies! The OTC cream I was using was doing something as I did not feel nearly as mean as I do usually this month, nor did I want to "eat the house" I think my mistake was assuming that my new pharmacy compounded meds were going to absorb the same and that it would be OK for me to go with a starting dose larger than my current OTC dose. I also tried to break it into a higher AM, lower PM dose and think I only managed to give myself a large headache the likes of which I have not had in years. The good news is that the Estrogen must surely be absorbing and I know I can't decrease from morning to evening by as much as I tried! I suspect this hormone replacement is going to take awhile to master, especially still being pre-menopause where all havoc can break loose from day to day hormone changes, this and the cyclical nature of the whole thing I find confusing. Colleen, if you are still reading this thread I'd love to hear your normal daily dose since you don't cycle plus what amount of cycling causes you issues. I think that I'm sensitive to dropping estrogen values, guess I will eventually figure out exactly how much of a drop will set them off...

### freshveggies — 2012-10-22T01:13:19+0300

So far the last 2 nights I haven't slept any worse by not taking my P. I keep a daily chart of what hormones I take, vitamins, my sleep, food and how I feel. It helps me figure out what is and what is not working.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-22T16:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-59625>

I started oral progesterone (50 mg) about 10 days ago and am having some odd symptoms that I think are probably related. I should note I haven't had a TOTM since February of this year and before that it was October of 2011, so they are few and far between. At only 35, I'm not sure if this is a blessing or a curse

Within days of starting the progesterone my nipples became very tender and sore, just like they used to get before I would get TOTM. My doctor did say he generally has patients take the prog for 2-3 weeks and then stop for a week to see if it comes. Because my levels were so crazy low he suggested I stay on for 4-5 weeks before stopping. The other issue I'm having is MAJOR heat issues. I don't think I'm having hot flashes per se, as these aren't short-lived episodes. I'll have days where I am burning up literally all day long.

I also started T3 a few days before the progesterone, but that's only 5 mcg of the timed release form and my T3 levels were also terribly low so I can't imagine 5 mcg would have me going hyper.

Has anyone else had the "heat episodes" from Prog? My BM's are getting a bit more difficult to pass too, and I have read that could be from the prog. Could it be a matter of my body finally getting what it needs and not being sure what to do with it right away? Maybe a transition period?

### LinD — 2012-10-23T04:58:03+0300

I just want to be optimal and have increased libido for DH. Awaiting to complete the saliva tests, but bloowork shows something isn't quite right...

TSH=1.780 (.450-4.5)

T3=2.3 (2.0-4.4)

T4, Free (direct)= 1.20 (.82-1.77)

Reverse T3, serum=25.5 (13.5-34.2)

Testosterone, Serum= 11 (3-41)

Lipoprotein (a)=16 (0-30)

Testosterone, Free (direct)=.2 (0.0-2.2)

Homocyst(e)ine, plasma=8.8 (0.0-15)

Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy=80.1 (30-100)

C-Reactive Protein=1.4 (0.0-4.9)

Clearly I have some work, but will BHRT make it worse before it's better?

Just wondering what I'm in for....

CT starting back up very soon since we finally got moved into the new house this weekend. (Though a whole Saturday was missed of unpacking while instead attending the TCU vs. Texas Tech football game--with three overtimes!). So we are getting our be home back together.

At this point we would need to see your progesterone and estrogen levels. Your FT3 is low. What is your ratio of Ft3 to Rt3. You can figure it out on STTM [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/)

Is this "F" t3 or just t3? Remember when doing the ratio, the result is different if you use t3 and not ft3. No matter what, you ratio is not good. Anytime the t3 is low and ft3 is high, it's a given.

As for bios, they might make it worse until you figure it all out. Like Jack says, that is a tough, very time consuming job and why most docs don't do what they should. If you need them, tis better to have them then to not. I'm working on my 4th doc for them now. One was willey nilley about it and would say "take more if you are grumpy", the next one said progesterone will "kill ya." The next would only test via saliva and no blood to see the course and now the next works with the anti-aging something or another and she puts out on her website clearly how she treats what so I won't be surprised when I go.

I am verifying though that she tests by blood.

Congrats on the move. Hope you get settled in soon. Tis a fun time, but also very exhausting. At least it is before the whole x-mas scene.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-25T23:05:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60189>

Need some input here, please! I started 50 mg oral Progesterone exactly two weeks ago and I'm noticing some major weight/water gain around my middle - hips, thighs and waist. I'm normally fairly thin, which I think kind of makes this more obvious and my pants are getting super tight.

My sleep has also gone to crap the last few nights and I'm still getting episodes of MAJOR overheating. Right now it's 64 degrees in my house and I'm in shorts and a tank top and my face is on fire! It's literally hot to the touch. Gretchen mentioned above that might be due to my thyroid turning on since I started 5 mcg of SR T3 a few weeks ago, but holy cow! If that's the case, how long will this last?

I wasn't able to get labs earlier this week when I wanted to, but I plan on going tomorrow AM for a full hormone and thyroid panel. My last set about a month ago showed super low/practically nil levels of all thyroid and sex hormones, so I wouldn't think these low doses would have such an effect!

I'm almost tempted to stop the Pg and T3 for a short while to see if things calm down. Thoughts?!?!

I bet you probably need more. Some pole have the side effect of weight gain when you don't have enough to counter act the E in your system as most Neolithic women are E dominant. Fwiw I'm still having the HOT thing going on. 100% thyroid for me

See where your labs are before stopping anything. I'd bet you need more T3 as well as Pg

### Jude — 2012-10-26T01:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60224>

> **freshveggies;61797 wrote:** I can get that way when the progesterone overrides the already low estrogen. They need to be in the right balance. Not sure if that is your case or not.

Can anyone refer me to some place that describes exactly the mechanism of correct P to E? I thought that ones body would love adapting to the 'normal' pattern of higher P to E? Dr K recommends 500 as the ratio but I don't understand that........nor does my GP...and I really need to be able to understand to argue the case.

### JanSz — 2012-10-26T02:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60240>

> **Jude;61831 wrote:** Can anyone refer me to some place that describes exactly the mechanism of correct P to E? I thought that ones body would love adapting to the 'normal' pattern of higher P to E? Dr K recommends 500 as the ratio but I don't understand that........nor does my GP...and I really need to be able to understand to argue the case.

I do not understand what dr K means when he says P/E ~500

But,

how about if you

1\. learn definition of P/E (it is a molar ratio)

2\. find out how to calculate P/E ratio, handy calculator is here: [http://new.instacalc.com/421](http://http://new.instacalc.com/421)

3\. forget (for a moment) about P/E ratio

4\. find out your proper P-progesterone level

5\. find ou your proper E-estrogen, E2-estradiol levels

6\. find out what kind P/E to expect when using proper (or idealized) P & E (they are far from 500)

7\. question again, what dr K means by P/E~500

\===

To find out (idealized) (good) progesterone and estradiol levels look here, look at solid line:

[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones\_estradiol,\_progesterone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol,_progesterone,_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-26T21:40:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60351>

For the ladies on Pg...I had a visit with my gyn and he said that the reason my Pg numbers aren't coming up is similar to T3 levels...your body uses the bioidentical Pg and it isn't stored. I don't know how you would ever get the Pg numbers up to get a good ratio if that is the case. Any ideas?

PS...he is not the dr. who is doing my BHRT.

### Pamela — 2012-10-29T05:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60672>

A word of warning or at least something to be aware of. I started BHRT through Dzugan in May. I've been going along taking all the prescribed creams and supplements. Last week I went to my endo who is helping me with my Thyroid and he took new blood work for all the hormones. He called me telling me my hormones were "wonky". My Serum Testosterone was sky high at 588 (range is 3-41), DHEA-S is 528 (range is 35.4-256). I have no weird symptoms so I would have never known.

A few weeks ago when I talked my Dzugan rep I asked specifically how often they request new blood work, she said once a year.

I feel this is really wrong and anyone using them should be testing probably every 3 months.

I just got my results so will be sending them in to them for adjusting but I felt all Dzugan people here should be aware.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-29T16:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60710>

I've been testing every quarter and suddenly the pattern has appeared. If I hadn't have tested in Sept/Oct - we would have never known that both my Thyroid needed additional support, and more Pg. the only indication beyond testing was an increase of migraines and constantly feeling like I was hot - such that my engine had turned on - but wasn't actively engaged.

We have to own our own health if we want to fix the problem... Jack has shown us that!

### BJK77 — 2012-10-29T18:48:42+0200

Interesting Destiny. I knew the PG:E2 ratio was important and if I recall I think the optimal # is around 500, but I didn't realize I could still technically be E dominant with such low levels. ### Shijin13 — 2012-10-30T19:03:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-60964>

> **freshveggies;62666 wrote:** HI Shijin, is there an online tracker of CM. I used to use the Creighton Model and they don't seem to have anything online.

I use the following: [www.fertilityfriend.com](http://http://www.fertilityfriend.com) I use it w/my iphone and track it on my phone...then when I remember I sync it... and its stored on their server...

probably not the friendliest program/app, but I started using it to track my cycle when we ditched all form of Birth control -and we started using the Fertility Awareness method for tracking cycling, ovulation and sex timing... you can pretty much put what ever info in the comments/daily notes section.

Need some suggestions. I am on Dzugan and do triest 1.25% all month long along with progesterone. During my luteal phase, I take

.2ml triest and 60mg progesterone. I do pretty good sleeping, then comes TOM and I am only sleeping 4 hours. I wake up around 2:30 and can't sleep. I have tried what they prescripbed. .1ml triest, 10mg prog. or .2ml triest and 20mg prog. Today I am trying .15ml triest and 30mg prog. I notice that I have more water retension during TOM. Is that low progesterone? If so, that is why I am trying more prog. I just don't want the balance off. Any suggestions?

### mafixit — 2012-11-19T15:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-65318>

Who is the best type of doctor to see for BHRT? We know my hormones are in the toilet based on the lab work I had in September.

Do I need to go to a specialized practice out of network? I know my PCP at Mayo won't handle this and my OBGYN keeps trying me on patches, which only make matters worse. I went off my BC Pills last week and now have had a continuous migraine for the past 5 days. My OBGYN put me on a patch instead and the migraine is worse than usual, now also at night (I used to be able to sleep it off). I stopped the patch last night and slept with ice on my neck, while waiting for her to call me about this issue.

Next week I see a new internist with Mayo who is double board certified in complementary medicine and then in January I see a Mayo Endocrinologist. Also waiting for results of ALCAT Food, Chemical, Herb and Mold Intolerance Testing.

In the meantime I am on:

1000 mg vitamin C

10 mg Lipitor

25 mg DHEA

100 mg CoQ10

1000 IU Vitamin D3

One a Day gummy vitamin

Pure Essence Labs Magnesium Plus

1 Viactiv Calcium Chew

Eating Paleo for the past 6 weeks or so. I have been very good. No refined foods, so sugars, no grains. Lost almost 5 lbs without intention.

Desperate for relief.

Karen from Arizona

### freshveggies — 2012-11-29T14:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-65850>

Dzugan question.Â  Do you all start your luteal phase hormones on the same day of of your cycle each month like day 15 or do you always count out TOM then go 10 days on the next dose then start the luteal dose?Â  Is that easy enough to follow?Â  Or do I need more explanation?

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-29T14:39:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-65853>

here's how I do it based off my cycle
TOTM is the 1st 4 days.(.7ml) Day 5 I start my 10day count of Pg & TriEst. (.8ml & .1ml triest) then day 15 I go to the luteal phase dose (1.0ml) until my next cycle starts. (which of recent has been about 24days)

make sense?

### freshveggies — 2012-11-29T15:05:35+0200

>

Â

### mafixit — 2012-12-06T10:45:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-66550>

I have spoken to both xrmd and dzlogic. I am debating between the two. Right now I am working on finding a doc here that will work with me on their treatment plans. Hope to have that in place today. They are both also providing some doctor options here in AZ. I prefer however to find someone in my plan who maybe has worked with BHRT.

Both have different upfront and ongoing costs, but the philosophy, testing and treatment seem to be the same. I liked talking to xrmd because I actually had a free consult with one of the doctors over Skype, which was quite informative. Xrmd is also talking about other genetic testing, which I am not opposed to.

Dzlogic wants a new baseline panel (my last was in Sept) and xrmd says the Sept lab is fine, but I think either way I will get a new panel since my insurance covers it, and since I started Paleo back in October. Plus my Day 21 will be a week from Monday, so the timing will be perfect.

Karen from Arizona

Progesterone will start on day 14 and increase through day 21 and then drop. Is there any problem dosing these higher levels of progesterone even though I didn't have high levels of estradiol earlier in the cycle? For what its worth, I did do 100mg vaginal suppositories of progesterone for a couple of cycles and that only made things better with the exception of the vaginal itching. Thanks for any input you can provide.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-07T14:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-66706>

Karen, one thing to consider is how aggressive will the OBGYN be versus the Dzugan or other anti-aging doc?Â  That for me has been the big difference.Â  I can feel better with just a little of this and a dab of that but if I really want to get to a great place it seems I have to find a doc that is more willing to dose higher.Â  That has been my experience anyway.

I think the best way to handle it with insurance is to do what Shijin does and have an insurance doc manage the Dzugan program.Â  That is using the system to your advantage!

### mafixit — 2012-12-14T09:34:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-67487>

> **Patty Cakes said**
> Karen, one thing to consider is how aggressive will the OBGYN be versus the Dzugan or other anti-aging doc?Â  That for me has been the big difference.Â  I can feel better with just a little of this and a dab of that but if I really want to get to a great place it seems I have to find a doc that is more willing to dose higher.Â  That has been my experience anyway.
>
> I think the best way to handle it with insurance is to do what Shijin does and have an insurance doc manage the Dzugan program.Â  That is using the system to your advantage!

You are right.Â  After my consult I was disappointed in the lack of aggressiveness of the Nurse Practitioner.Â  I showed her the article regarding Dzugan and his program and she looked frazzled and said "I don't know anything about that", and was not interested in keeping it to read.Â  Her approach was to leave me on BC pills and add testosterone and Vitamin D3, then in 6 weeks see how I feel and draw new labs.Â  She also told me that the timing of my labs did not matter, since I technically do not have a cycle since I am on BC pills.Â  Not sure if there is any truth to that or not.

Her opinion was that my dose of synthetic hormones (Generess FE currently) was larger than anything she would prescribe and opted to leave me on them.Â  If I go off, even for a little while, I crash and burn and end up curled up in a ball with even more severe migraines and other female issues. And actually since my other OB reduced my pills this this dose, I have had more and more headaches.

But what really turned me off is when I asked about my lack of sleep and taking Melatonin.Â  She shook her head and said "no", which is fine, but then she actually mentioned that she saw something on Dr. Oz about how melatonin is bad for you.Â  Yes, she actually told me she saw it on Dr. Oz.Â  There are so many differing opinions.Â  My headache neuro says 9 mg a night, OBGYN Nurse says none, Dr. Oz says 1 mg at the most, and then only for short term.

She was nice and caring, but not quite what I was hoping for.Â 

I am going to talk to the PCP that Dzugan is recommending for me here to administer their plan.Â  I am on the fence, but falling over slowing to the DZLogic side, especially since I am on a 2 day migraine (it's cycle end).

At this point I am on no supplements, or vitamins until I get some guidance.Â  Just the BC pills and Lipitor.

Â 

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-14T09:48:13+0200

Â 

its frustrating... Go w/DzLogic - it will become the best $ you've ever spent. Â I've got a great working relationship w/my adviser. Â  my first recommendation is get of the BPCs Â and ditch the lipitor - my LDL has dropped like a stone since I've been on the BHRT - btwn the thyroid and Pg - my body is now clearing what I've been making... is it still high by CW standards - yeah but in the end I'm seeing progress ....Â

### Ria — 2012-12-23T09:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-68236>

Has anyone else gotten severe acne from the bio-identical progesterone? Â 

I am 12 years post menopause, after lurking here for quite a while I finally found someone in the area who does the bio-identical stuff. Â According to the zrt lab, I have only a trace of p in my system, but my e is in the low normal pre-men range. Â But 36 hours after starting supplementation, I get cystic acne. Â Doc's thought was that my other supplementation of DHEA and pregnenolone was causing it. Â Years ago prescribed DHEA did cause acne, but the 7-keto does not and my levels are finally up to low normal on that. Â So I stopped both the 7-keto and the preg and still the acne. Â As soon as I stop the progesterone, the acne disappears rapidly. Take one dose and 8 hours later acne is back. So what is up? Â And more importantly, what do I do? Â The main reason to do the bhrt is for my bones (-3.9 spine on the dexa) and arthritis. Â Already estrogen dominant, so I don't want to take just the estradiol w/o progesterone.

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

This has been my experience... and my Pg:E2 ratio is no where it needs to be yet... but we're working on getting there...

### SeaJane — 2013-01-05T11:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-69414>

I started BHRT this month based on the Wiley protocol. My ND and I made an error when calculating the total Estradiol needed for the month so I got to day 13 when I ran out. I ordered more and started back up on the protocol on evening of day 15. So this meant I missed 2.5 days of dosing estradiol although I started the progesterone on day 14 as scheduled. On Day 16, I woke to some brown spotting so I continued with the protocol hoping it would stop and I'd finish the month without incident. So the flow has picked up some overnight and there is bright red blood as well. My question is do I continue and go through the progesterone half of the cycle or do I stop and restart the estrogen? Is this because of the fast drop in estrogen or something else I need to consider so I can adjust for the next month. I am peri-menopausal and cycling regularly. Thanks.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-07T12:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-69744>

Don't know what it is about the winter months but things always seem to go awry for me during this time. My thyroid anitbodies are low so I do not believe its a biotoxin as much as I would have in the past (last winter they were off the charts and it coincided with me feeling awful).

I got my thyroid results back and basically had to come off of my Cytomel (leaving just my armour dose). My Free T3 levels were too high so thats why they are doing that.

Then around this time, my boob have gone up at least a cup size and are tender. Wish I could enjoy those things, but I can't. I'd like my b-cups back! I can't fit into any of my clothes.

I'm only on progesterone at this point. Dzugan just ordered a new hormone workup for me so am eager to get those results (drew the labs this saturday - have no idea how long it takes to get them back? a week?).

I have def gained weight and just feel tired again. Maybe I'm on too my P now too? Sigh. Just when I was in a groove.

Why would the weather cause such an uprising in my body?!

Sorry, but this is just depressing

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-10T13:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-70164>

Wiley protocol looks like this:

Estrogen E-2: 1.0mg/o.1ml cream (1 line on a 3ml #9 syringe)
Day 1-5: 0.4ml (4 lines) TWICE daily
Day 6-8: 0.6ml (6 lines) TWICE daily
Day 9-11: 0.7ml (7 lines) TWICE daily
Day 12: 0.8ml (8 lines) TWICE
Day 13-14: 0.2 ml (2 lines) TWICE daily
Day 15-17: 0.3ml (3 lines) TWICE daily
Day 18-28: 0.4ml (4lines) TWICE daily

Day 12 is the full moon which is how you figure out how many "baseline" days you have in a cycle. Those days are always 0.4ml (4 lines) TWICE a day.

Progesterone P4: 20mg/0.1ml cream (1 line on a 3ml #9 syringe)
Day 1-13: ZERO
Day 14-15: 0.4ml (4 lines) TWICE daily
Day 16-17: 0.8ml (8 lines) TWICE daily
Day 18-19: 1.0ml (10 lines) TWICE daily
Day 20: 1.2ml (12 lines) TWICE
Day 21: 1.4ml (14 lines) TWICE
Day 22: 1.2 (12 lines) TWICE
Day 23-24: 1.0ml (10 lines) TWICE daily
Day 25-26: 0.8ml (8 lines) TWICE daily
Day 27-28: 0.4ml (4 lines) TWICE daily

I'd suggest printing out this schedule and the amounts and showing it to your doc. Your pharmacist should compound to these specs so then you'll never wonder if you got the dose right. If you need help figuring out the calendar just let me know. It's not difficult at all once you can pinpoint the date of the full moon.

I print out a monthly calendar and put the dose for each on the specific day. Then, in the morning I make half an "X" through that dose so I know I've used it and then finish the "X" in the evening. I tape the calendar to my bathroom mirror so I don't forget a dose (99.9% of the time) and I know just where I am.

HTHs

### SueC — 2013-01-12T17:28:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-70439>

Are there any of you out there who were postmenopausal when you started BHRT? If so, would you talk a little about your experience? I'll be 60 this year and I've used OTC progesterone cream for years but I'm wondering about doing a more complete BHRT program.

### caroline — 2013-01-12T18:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-70446>

Hi Sue! I am post menopausal .... After a consult with Dr. K in September - I started progesterone and DHEA and pregnenolone..... this was determined by my ZRT/cortisol testing and blood work - per JK. My progesterone was critically low and my PG/E ratio was horrible so JK suggested dosing. A few days after this consult I found a lovely new Doc who listened to the consult and was happy to play along and learn with me with Jack's guidance. I have just had another consult and my pregesterone has come up a little and my PD/E ratio has come up. Dr K. now suggested - for my situation - to hit it really hard with Progesterone for 6-8 weeks and then re test. My Doc concurs after discussing with me how I feel about it.....so I can keep you posted. I am presently using oral troches and vaginal and cream and we shall see....
BTW - prior to my first testing and first consult - I thought I was pretty okay. I was pretty sure that I had adrenal fatigue because of many years of looking after a very ill husband - and the docs here only do minimal testing and said I was fine. Dr K. said I had been living in bad for so long that I didn't know what bad was ....how true I now realize! It is ridiculous listening to my first consult again - I kept saying I was fine!! I guess I was hoping for the power of positive thinking!!
So the moral is - you definitely need testing to see where you are at and get a baseline and then Dr. K. - along with a partner Doc - can help get you on track. If your testing is okay then you win the lottery! Good Luck on this crazy journey - I am loving every second - once again - it is this crazy \[meant in the nicest possible way

\] guy with a flashlight

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-13T15:32:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-70544>

Sue...I'm post menopausal at 54 (tomorrow!) I was done with periods by the time I was 44, so it's been some years. I never did any hormone replacement until this summer. Started DHEA first and then found a Dr. who put me on biest at a very low dose and some T. all in the same cream. I've not liked this much at all mostly because I can't really experiment. I've upped my dose by half and will be testing this month. She only wanted to see me every six months, so she's not a tweaker, I've added pregnenolone on my own as well. I feel like the thyroid replacement that I started at the same time has given me more specific results. The hormones...not so much. My plan is to ask my Dr to separate the creams and see what effect these low doses have had on my labs. I'm generally feeling way better than I was six months ago so it's all going in the right direction, I just think I would thrive with a little more help from the hormones. I've got to test my adrenals again too...I think they are functioning much better.

BJ...... prometrium is bio identical progesterone - available in the U.S. Is that where you are? anyway - you can order it from - drugstore4save - without a prescription .... and it can be used orally or vaginally. It was what Dr. K. recommended for me. It is a small round capsule that is micronized and has a gel coating so will dissolve in the vagina. if you want more info just let me know. The one thing I don't understand is why sometimes a vaginal cream is suggested and why sometimes a troche is suggested. Dr. K. recommended a vaginal troche to me but I said the prometrium says it can be used either way \[says on box\] so he said to go ahead and use it.

### SeaJane — 2013-01-16T10:51:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71029>

I just started BHRT this month and I am experiencing weight gain although I am not sure if this is due to hormones alone or low FT3 or both. I just doubled my T3 levels the last 2 days (up from 7.5mcg twice a day to 15 mcg twice a day) and am feeling much better. I am no longer constantly cold and the Raynaud's symptoms have gone way down. I am also taking d-ribose, coQ10, acetly-carnitine, and magnesium for mito support.

Is weight gain associated with progesterone, estrogen, or the ratio? When I just did progesterone with vaginal troches during second half of cycle, I did not gain weight. Also, when I did progesterone cream at about 200mg/day for second half of cycle I did not get weight gain. Only this cycle where I am cycling estrogen and progesterone the whole time per the Wiley protocol and the progesterone levels are much higher than earlier. I don't have any other symptoms (i.e., tender breasts, depressed, brain fog) and my sleep has been good. Anyways, as I write this out, I realize this is questions for the BHRT thread probably and will post it over there as well.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-16T11:01:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71032>

I did my day 21 ratio and its 76.5 - what is an ideal ratio of P to E?

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-16T11:14:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71033>

> **nuttmegs17 said**
>
> I did my day 21 ratio and its 76.5 - what is an ideal ratio of P to E?
> thanks!

pg:e2 ration should be btwn 100-500. Jack prefers in top Quartile, so you want it above 400.

mine started out at 5, when I was doing the LR back in 2011, in Jan 12 it was 4.7, in may12 it was 76 (after starting pg supplementation), and back in Oct12 it was 104 - at least I was in range... looking fwd to seeing what it looks like now that we've pushed the Pg really high (I need more though)

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-16T15:43:06+0200

Caroline

I test every quarter for all BIG LABS (homones, Lipids, hsCRP, IGF-1) & I test every 4-6 wks for thyroid. some times if things indicate we need to test more regularly we will but I leave it to the equinoxes, and solstices

I'm sitting here at work - feeling sluggish, and over all crappy and I couldn't figure out why... until I realized that in the chaos that this morning was (Chaos & Havoc - where being quite the little pistols!) that I forgot my am dose of Pg!!!! ugggghhhhh DO. NOT. LIKE!

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-17T16:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71260>

Caroline

I take mine 2x/day - I'm taking a compounded gel - at this point in my cycle I'm taking 10% Pg gel @1.4ml/day - I'm supposed to split the dose btwn the am and pm... OMG the brain fog, sucks... I generally don't feel well.

Dzugan starts w/the gels, if you don't bring your Pg up - then he starts looking at oral and Vaginal pg. depending how my labs look - I'm probably going to ask to consider adding "prometrium" vaginally - b/c I'm not 100% sure I'm a good absorber of the gels - b/c of my high T levels from my PCOS - my understanding is T can block trans-dermal absorption... but I'm still trying to figure that out...

Both Dzugan and Wiley advocate compounded gels - from what I've seen...

### Claudia — 2013-01-19T20:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71712>

Has anyone here heard of taking oral prometrium caps and inserting them vaginally. I was trying to get more information about using a progesterone suppository. I have read that Dr. Mercola recommends vaginally as the best method and Dr. Kruse actually just said this in this past week's Q&A. Dr. K recommends Prometrium vaginally. He did not explain why but now there are two doctors whom I respect saying the same thing.
I have a Rx for oral. I'd rather not add to the burden of my liver. So, I read online that women are inserting the oral caps vaginally. Seems a bit odd to me. Don't they make something better. I'm going to keep googling

.

THanks.

### Claudia — 2013-01-19T22:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71737>

Just saw what's in oral prometrium and not crazy about taking that orally or vaginally (unless the vaginal form is different?)

The inactive ingredients for PROMETRIUM Capsules 100 mg include: peanut oil NF, gelatin NF, glycerin USP, lecithin NF, titanium dioxide USP, D&C Yellow No. 10, and FD&C Red No. 40.

Anyone else care about this? I don't like the idea of ingesting titanium dioxide and yellow and red dye. Just so the pill can be pink?

I really don't know what to do now. I guess I'll try an over the counter cream. There are so many though so it's difficult to choose.

### caroline — 2013-01-20T01:03:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-71755>

I asked at my compounding pharmacy and she told me if it is micronized and has a gel coating - it should work. In Australia - troches are used vaginally but since I had these I am using them

### Claudia — 2013-01-21T23:01:53+0200

> **caroline said**

I asked at my compounding pharmacy and she told me if it is micronized and has a gel coating - it should work. In Australia - troches are used vaginally but since I had these I am using them

Well, I just posted that I heard Dr. K recommend it vaginally on the Q&A the other night. I was wondering what everyone thought of this.

I had a compounding pharmacy make me a cream recently and my doc, who didn't seem to know what she was doing, prescribed 200 mg dose for me. I had terrible side effects from it, just horrible mental and physical. I am perimeno and not even that low in P so it was a ridiculous dose for me. But I am bit scared now of compounded P. I tried it the next cycle and used just a tiny amount of the spoonful, 1/4 of it, aiming for 50mg dose, and that was still too much...all the symptoms started coming back after a couple of days and I just discontinued it altogether.

When I tried the oral prometrium before I didn't have any side effects, dose was 100mg. Maybe oral is better for me, but I just don't like the idea of swallowing dyes and fillers and thought I'd find like-minded people on here and some ideas on what else to do. I like the oilve oil base idea. Maybe I'll ask about that.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-23T19:48:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-72578>

This isn't related to your question but just something I found helpful for me. I was on a lot of compounded estrogen and still getting hot flashes, day and night, even after many months of treatment (also on a lot of progesterone). I started a supplement called Estrovera and that has really worked for me. For a while i was taking 2-3/day but have been able to back it down to 1. I am going to once again try to reduce estrogen dosing little by little as I know my Pg/E2 balance is off and I'm hoping this supp will let me do it.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-23T20:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-72583>

This is the second time in my life w/hot flashes. I stopped using Estrogel years ago and didn't have any other problems until just recently after clearing all the excess I had going on. My Pg/E2 ration was below 1!!! So I really don't want to use any estrogen now but a dab may be helpful. Prior to Estrogel I used Estroven and it worked great! It was probably the black cohosh. But I did notice it has soy isoflavones and I don't eat soy at all because of the Hashis. I even look for soy lecithin in supps. The more you know the better you act and the harder it gets!!! LOL The health path we are traveling on just keeps getting thinner with all this info. I love it all and I am getting more skilled at the thin path anyway.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-23T22:14:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-72598>

> **Shijin13 said**
>
> fwiw - ime the hot flashes were a result of thyroid turning on,...and my mitochondria trying to move to fat burning... still not quite there yet though...

I've had both kinds of hot flashes and the estrogen-deficiency hot flashes feel different to me than those from excess thyroid (which I went through the last couple of months). With the estrogen HF, it was an overwhelming heat starting in my upper chest and rapidly working up to my head. I absolutely had to take off whatever possible layers I could ASAP. With the thyroid HF, it is less intense, seems to be more of a whole body effect, and many times I get the sense that I can just deal with it rather than disrobe (though I usually do throw off whatever layer I can). For a long while, I thought the hot flashes I was having were due to lack of estrogen so I was increasing biest with no effect. This is confusing stuff!

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-29T19:01:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-73171>

> [freshveggies said:](/goto/post?id=73169)
>
> for the women taking estrogen and low in estrogen how do you know when you have enough? I think I am low, but sometimes afraid of pushing it higher? I can't sleep and I think low E is an issue. My Last estradiol was 300 on day 19 and P was 10. I know P is low, but I think E is low too. Breasts are not filled out.

I'm unsure about that. My breasts actually went up a size when I increased P (which I can say it was welcomed, but they hurt so its not any fun!), so you may still need to work on P. I found its not always as simple as "E equals bigger boobs, P equals water loss". Its about the ratios but Im not very good at figuring those out. Hopefully one of the experts will chime in!

### diane — 2013-02-06T18:59:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-74451>

Gretchen it's because of you and the rest of the people here that fight for what they know is right vs. what their doctor says - well that's also given me the courage and ability to start doing that. Plus I'm starting to understand this all better, so have more rationale when I do ask. I'm trying to increase my thyroid meds every 2 weeks rather than every 4 with testing - the doc came back and said no, but I will get my wish. No only means she doesn't get it yet. Am also getting my own RT3 done even though she says she doesn't use it - I'm gonna show her what it can be used for. Next - progesterone!!

### kathylu — 2013-02-06T19:26:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-74454>

Well girls, all I can say is the "educating your doctor" thing is starting to bear fruit. I wanted to up my progesterone (double it, so I dropped off a hand written note to my doctor asking her to rewrite my script as "Progesterone cream, 100 mg/ml, 1 ml bid, dispense 60 ml, 4 refills" and 5 minutes later she walked to the front desk, handed me the script and said "Let me know how it goes, ok?"

Too easy!

### vkiernan — 2013-02-06T20:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-74468>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=74451)
>
> Gretchen it's because of you and the rest of the people here that fight for what they know is right vs. what their doctor says - well that's also given me the courage and ability to start doing that. Plus I'm starting to understand this all better, so have more rationale when I do ask. I'm trying to increase my thyroid meds every 2 weeks rather than every 4 with testing - the doc came back and said no, but I will get my wish. No only means she doesn't get it yet. Am also getting my own RT3 done even though she says she doesn't use it - I'm gonna show her what it can be used for. Next - progesterone!!

You can get what you want if you know what you are talking about and have a backup plan for unexpected reults. In other words, let them know that if "this' or "this" happens you will do "this." This tells them you have done your research and are prepared for the worst. You let them know you will monitor results and do x accordingly if needed. Pretty soon your doc will trust you and learn something too. It doesn't hurt to tell them you will check in periodically as well by e-mail or whatever works.

### caroline — 2013-02-06T22:04:55+0200

To me that is everything - Your Doctor has to trust that you are doing the research and you are responsible and will keep him in the loop. ### diane — 2013-03-01T01:38:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-78423>

Ok - I got my hormone results - finally!

My progesterone is 1.1 nmol/L (ref range 5.3 - 86). It converts to 3.5 ng/ml. I had it taken on day 21 of my cycle. I'm thinking that this is in the toilet! Estradiol is 340 pmol/L (ref range 151-774) converts to 1248 pg/ml (does that make sense?). My Pg is down from a year ago, E is up slightly.

So when I see my doctor on Monday, I need to ask for compounded Pg, and I'm assuming a lot of it! Any advice in creating my argument would be helpful. I'm not sure she's ready for the PPP explanation. LOL.

On a positive note, had my DHEA-S tested again, and it's up from 4.6 to 5.2 in 2 months. I'm only on 10mg of DHEA, but it's prescription which I'm guessing may be better quality than the 25mg - 50mg I was taking before. I want to ask for more, but with the increase, may be a hard sell lol.

### MsYo — 2013-03-01T21:50:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-78531>

From reading this blog/forum, I have managed to avoid a hysterectomy, shrink my fibroids, develop a great working relationship with my primary care doctor, fire my ob/gyn, and improve my sleep. My last appointment I got updated hormone panel, bloodwork and thyroid testing all without any resistance. I discussed my reasoning for the request and he agreed. He also offered alternative ideas for me to consider. Next up.... new ASI labs and getting copies of my last labwork. "Normal" isn't enough details for me so I'll go in for a new visit. I wrote all of this to say, it can be done..... DON'T GIVE UP or GET DISCOURAGED.

### Claudia — 2013-03-02T00:34:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-78542>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=78423)
>
> Ok - I got my hormone results - finally!
>
> My progesterone is 1.1 nmol/L (ref range 5.3 - 86). It converts to 3.5 ng/ml. I had it taken on day 21 of my cycle. I'm thinking that this is in the toilet! Estradiol is 340 pmol/L (ref range 151-774) converts to 1248 pg/ml (does that make sense?). My Pg is down from a year ago, E is up slightly.
>
> So when I see my doctor on Monday, I need to ask for compounded Pg, and I'm assuming a lot of it! Any advice in creating my argument would be helpful. I'm not sure she's ready for the PPP explanation. LOL.
>
> On a positive note, had my DHEA-S tested again, and it's up from 4.6 to 5.2 in 2 months. I'm only on 10mg of DHEA, but it's prescription which I'm guessing may be better quality than the 25mg - 50mg I was taking before. I want to ask for more, but with the increase, may be a hard sell lol.

Be careful. Why do you think you need a lot of compounded pg? Start slowly and see how it goes. In a normal cycle, your body produces 25mg per day of progesterone. Shouldn't we all stay close to that amount when using bioidentical progesterone? I had a BAD experience recently with progesterone cream compounded, very BAD. And my doc started me at 200mg cream, way TOO high for me. I'm scared of it all now. I know I need some but if I start again it's going to be 20mg per day.

I know several other women who have had bad experiences, including weight gain and feeling terrible, because of progesterone creams. I know some who have bad experiences with DHEA, too. I seem to recall Dr. Kruse saying to go with 7-KETO-DHEA if you're a woman. Diane, what is the name of your Rx for DHEA, a particular brand? Sounds like it's increasing for you. Again, I'm not sure why you want to increase it. Why not just go slow? IF you increase things too fast, you could have side effects that you don't want and then, what do you do?

### diane — 2013-03-02T01:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-advice-suggestions-and.1633/post-78551>

Hi Claudia. I absolutely see your point about not going too fast. Definitely for the progesterone I have to feel it out as I've never been on it before. My doc asked me if I wanted a prescription for cream the last time I was there, but I didn't have my labs, and started on thyroid so wanted to do one thing at a time. My comment about getting it up is linked to the discussion that we've been having that some women need even higher levels of progesterone than their doctor realizes. But agree - I don't want to push it too fast and get bad effects.

For the DHEA - it was made at a compounding pharmacy, so not sure what kind it is. I've been on DHEA since August, so if 10 puts it up a little bit, I'm willing to push it more and see if sleep, etc improves.

I guess I"m figuring if I can increase it, and feel bad affects, I'll just reduce it. But you definitely reinforced that it's good to have smaller increments so you can back down if you need it.

Thanks for sharing your experience Claudia! And I look forward to more of your input!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-13T00:44:56+0200

Andee you may have changed the pH of your vagina. I heard a doc talk recently about how depending on where we are in our cycle the pH can change. I was on Chasteberry (Vitex) for 3 years before starting with the progesterone cream. Chasteberry worked to a certain degree with my breast cyst (breasts still lumpy) but was less and less effective with my PMS. So I started to look for another solution. The solution was progesterone that made my breast soft and my PMS bearable.

My own experience was that Chasteberry is helpful but not as effective as progesterone.

Others can chime in here but you need enough estrogen too. Not everyone is so estrogen dominant. Some women are low in estrogen. I am very low and can't function without it. Many other issues too, but just saying progesterone doesn't fix all. Then on the other hand, most women are probably progesterone deficient.

Not saying that just adding P will add to more tummy, but being low in E can lead to more tummy ---as your body gets older and isn't making E as much, you need fat to make E and that is one reason why women gain weight so that their bodies can produce enough E.
